text.KJV: And my soul recovered the spirit of understanding, and I began to talk with the most High again,
text.NRSV: Then my soul recovered the spirit of understanding, and I began once more to speak words in the presence of the Most High.

text.DRB: and I said: Lord Dominator of every wood of the earth, and all the trees thereof, thou hast chosen one vineyard:
text.KJV: And said, O Lord that bearest rule, of every wood of the earth, and of all the trees thereof, thou hast chosen thee one only vine:
text.NRSV: I said, ‘O sovereign Lord, from every forest of the earth and from all its trees you have chosen one vine,

text.DRB: and of every land of the world thou hast chosen thee one ditch: and of all the flowers of the world thou hast chosen thee one lily:
text.KJV: And of all lands of the whole world thou hast chosen thee one pit: and of all the flowers thereof one lily:
text.NRSV: and from all the lands of the world you have chosen for yourself one region,] and from all the flowers of the world you have chosen for yourself one lily,

text.DRB: and of all depths of the sea, thou has filled thee one river: & of all the builded cities, thou hast sanctified unto thyself Sion:
text.KJV: And of all the depths of the sea thou hast filled thee one river: and of all builded cities thou hast hallowed Sion unto thyself:
text.NRSV: and from all the depths of the sea you have filled for yourself one river, and from all the cities that have been built you have consecrated Zion for yourself,

text.DRB: and of all created souls, thou hast named thee
text.KJV: And of all the fowls that are created thou hast named thee one dove: and of all the cattle that are made thou hast provided thee one sheep:
text.NRSV: and from all the birds that have been created you have named for yourself one dove, and from all the flocks that have been made you have provided for yourself one sheep,

text.DRB: and of all multiplied peoples, thou hast purchased thee one people: and a law approved of all thou hast given to this people, whom thou didst desire.
text.KJV: And among all the multitudes of people thou hast gotten thee one people: and unto this people, whom thou lovedst, thou gavest a law that is approved of all.
text.NRSV: and from all the multitude of peoples you have taken for yourself one people; and to this people, whom you have loved, you have given the law that is approved by all.

text.DRB: And now Lord, why hast thou delivered one unto many? And thou had prepared upon one root others, and hast dispersed thy only one in many:
text.KJV: And now, O Lord, why hast thou given this one people over unto many? and upon the one root hast thou prepared others, and why hast thou scattered thy only one people among many?
text.NRSV: And now, O Lord, why have you handed the one over to the many, and dishonoured] the one root beyond the others, and scattered your only one among the many?

text.DRB: and they have trodden upon it, which gainsaid thy covenants, and which believed not thy testaments.
text.KJV: And they which did gainsay thy promises, and believed not thy covenants, have trodden them down.
text.NRSV: And those who opposed your promises have trampled on those who believed your covenants.

text.DRB: And if hating thou hatest thy people, it ought to be chastised with thy hands
text.KJV: If thou didst so much hate thy people, yet shouldest thou punish them with thine own hands.
text.NRSV: If you really hate your people, they should be punished at your own hands.’

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when I had spoken the words, and the Angel was sent to me, that came to me before the night past,
text.KJV: Now when I had spoken these words, the angel that came to me the night afore was sent unto me,
text.NRSV: When I had spoken these words, the angel who had come to me on a previous night was sent to me.

text.DRB: & he said to me: Hear me, & I will instruct thee: and harken to me, and I will add before thee.
text.KJV: And said unto me, Hear me, and I will instruct thee; hearken to the thing that I say, and I shall tell thee more.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Listen to me, and I will instruct you; pay attention to me, and I will tell you more.’

text.KJV: And I said, Speak on, my Lord. Then said he unto me, Thou art sore troubled in mind for Israel’s sake: lovest thou that people better than he that made them?
text.NRSV: Then I said, “No, my lord.” And he said to me, “Are you greatly distracted in mind over Israel? Do you love him more than his Maker does?”
text.DRB: And I said: Speak my Lord. And he said to me: Thou art become exceedingly in excess of mind for Israel: hast thou loved it more than him that made it?
And I said to him: No Lord, but for sorrow I have spoken, for my veins torment me every hour, to apprehend the path of the Highest, and to search part of his judgement.
text.KJV: And I said, No, Lord: but of very grief have I spoken: for my reins pain me every hour, while I labour to comprehend the way of the most High, and to seek out part of his judgment.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘No, my lord, but because of my grief I have spoken; for every hour I suffer agonies of heart, while I strive to understand the way of the Most High and to search out some part of his judgement.’

text.DRB: And he said to me: Thou canst not. And I said: Why Lord? To what was I born, or why was not my mother's womb my grave, that I might not see the labour of Jacob, and the weariness of the stock of Israel?
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Thou canst not. And I said, Wherefore, Lord? whereunto was I born then? or why was not my mother’s womb then my grave, that I might not have seen the travail of Jacob, and the wearisome toil of the stock of Israel?
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘You cannot.’ And I said, ‘Why not, my lord? Why then was I born? Or why did not my mother’s womb become my grave, so that I would not see the travail of Jacob and the exhaustion of the people of Israel?’

text.DRB: And he said to me: Number me the things that are not yet come, and gather me the dispersed drops, and make me the withered flowers green again,
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Number me the things that are not yet come, gather me together the dross that are scattered abroad, make me the flowers green again that are withered,
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Count up for me those who have not yet come, and gather for me the scattered raindrops, and make the withered flowers bloom again for me;

text.DRB: & open me the shut cellars, & bring me forth the blasts inclosed in them, shew me the image of a voice: & then will I shew thee the labour that thou desirest to see.
text.KJV: Open me the places that are closed, and bring me forth the winds that in them are shut up, shew me the image of a voice: and then I will declare to thee the thing that thou labourest to know.
text.NRSV: open for me the closed chambers, and bring out for me the winds shut up in them, or show me the picture of a voice; and then I will explain to you the travail that you ask to understand.’]

text.DRB: And I said: Lord Dominator, for who is there that can know these things, but he that hath not his habitation with men?
text.KJV: And I said, O Lord that bearest rule, who may know these things, but he that hath not his dwelling with men?
text.NRSV: I said, ‘O sovereign Lord, who is able to know these things except him whose dwelling is not with mortals?

text.DRB: And I am unwise, and how can I speak of these things, which thou hast asked me?
text.KJV: As for me, I am unwise: how may I then speak of these things whereof thou askest me?
text.NRSV: As for me, I am without wisdom, and how can I speak concerning the things that you have asked me?’

text.DRB: And he said to me: As thou canst not do one of these things, which have been said: so canst thou not find my judgement, or in the end the charity, which I have promised to the people.
text.KJV: Then said he unto me, Like as thou canst do none of these things that I have spoken of, even so canst thou not find out my judgment, or in the end the love that I have promised unto my people.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Just as you cannot do one of the things that were mentioned, so you cannot discover my judgement, or the goal of the love that I have promised to my people.’

text.DRB: And I said: But behold Lord thou art nigh to them that are near the end: and what shall they do that have been before me, or we, or they after us?
text.KJV: And I said, Behold, O Lord, yet art thou nigh unto them that be reserved till the end: and what shall they do that have been before me, or we that be now, or they that shall come after us?
text.NRSV: I said, ‘Yet, O Lord, you have charge of those who are alive at the end, but what will those do who lived before me, or we, ourselves, or those who come after us?’

text.DRB: And he said to me: I will resemble my judgement to a crown. As there shall not be slackness of the last, so neither swiftness of the former.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, I will liken my judgment unto a ring: like as there is no slackness of the last, even so there is no swiftness of the first.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘I shall liken my judgement to a circle;] just as for those who are last there is no slowness, so for those who are first there is no haste.’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: Couldst thou not make them that have been, & that are, and that shall be, at once, that thou mayst shew thy judgement the quicker?
text.KJV: So I answered and said, Couldest thou not make those that have been made, and be now, and that are for to come, at once; that thou mightest shew thy judgment the sooner?
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘Could you not have created at one time those who have been and those who are and those who will be, so that you might show your judgement the sooner?’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: The creature cannot hasten above the Creator, nor the world sustain them that are to be created in it, at once.
text.KJV: Then answered he me, and said, The creature may not haste above the maker; neither may the world hold them at once that shall be created therein.
text.NRSV: He replied to me and said, ‘The creation cannot move faster than the Creator, nor can the world hold at one time those who have been created in it.’

text.DRB: And I said: As thou didst say to thy servant, that quickening thou didst quicken the creature created by thee at once, and the creature sustained it: it may now also bear them present at once.
text.KJV: And I said, As thou hast said unto thy servant, that thou, which givest life to all, hast given life at once to the creature that thou hast created, and the creature bare it: even so it might now also bear them that now be present at once.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘How have you said to your servant that you] will certainly give life at one time to your creation? If therefore all creatures will live at one time] and the creation will sustain them, it might even now be able to support all of them present at one time.’

text.DRB: And he said to me: Ask the matrice of a woman, and thou shalt say to it: And if thou bring forth children, why by times? Ask it therefore, that it give ten at once.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Ask the womb of a woman, and say unto her, If thou bringest forth children, why dost thou it not together, but one after another? pray her therefore to bring forth ten children at once.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Ask a woman’s womb, and say to it, “If you bear ten] children, why one after another?” Request it therefore to produce ten at one time.’

text.DRB: And I said, it cannot verily: but according to time.
text.KJV: And I said, She cannot: but must do it by distance of time.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘Of course it cannot, but only each in its own time.’

text.DRB: And he said to me: And I have given a matrice to the earth for them, that are sown upon it by time.
text.KJV: Then said he unto me, Even so have I given the womb of the earth to those that be sown in it in their times.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Even so I have given the womb of the earth to those who from time to time are sown in it.

text.DRB: For as the infant bringeth not forth the things that pertain to the aged, so have I disposed the world created of me.
text.KJV: For like as a young child may not bring forth the things that belong to the aged, even so have I disposed the world which I created.
text.NRSV: For as an infant does not bring forth, and a woman who has become old does not bring forth any longer, so I have made the same rule for the world that I created.’

text.DRB: And I asked, and said: Whereas thou hast now given me a way, I will speak before thee: for our mother, of whom thou toldest me, yet she is young: now draweth nigh to old age.
text.KJV: And I asked, and said, Seeing thou hast now given me the way, I will proceed to speak before thee: for our mother, of whom thou hast told me that she is young, draweth now nigh unto age.
text.NRSV: Then I inquired and said, ‘Since you have now given me the opportunity, let me speak before you. Is our mother, of whom you have told me, still young? Or is she now approaching old age?’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Ask her that beareth children, and she will tell thee.
text.KJV: He answered me, and said, Ask a woman that beareth children, and she shall tell thee.
text.NRSV: He replied to me, ‘Ask a woman who bears children, and she will tell you.

text.DRB: For thou shalt say to her: Why are not they whom thou hast brought forth now like to them that were before thee, but less of stature?
text.KJV: Say unto her, Wherefore are unto they whom thou hast now brought forth like those that were before, but less of stature?
text.NRSV: Say to her, “Why are those whom you have borne recently not like those whom you bore before, but smaller in stature?”

text.DRB: And she also will say unto thee: They that are born in the youth of strength are of one sort, and they of another, that are born about the time of old age, when the matrice faileth.
text.KJV: And she shall answer thee, They that be born in the the strength of youth are of one fashion, and they that are born in the time of age, when the womb faileth, are otherwise.
text.NRSV: And she herself will answer you, “Those born in the strength of youth are different from those born during the time of old age, when the womb is failing.”

text.DRB: Consider therefore thou also, that you are of less stature, than they that were before you:
text.KJV: Consider thou therefore also, how that ye are less of stature than those that were before you.
text.NRSV: Therefore you also should consider that you and your contemporaries are smaller in stature than those who were before you,

text.DRB: and they that are after you, of lesser than you, as it were creatures now waxing old, and past the strength of youth.
text.KJV: And so are they that come after you less than ye, as the creatures which now begin to be old, and have passed over the strength of youth.
text.NRSV: and those who come after you will be smaller than you, as born of a creation that already is ageing and passing the strength of youth.’

text.DRB: And I said: I beseech thee Lord, if I have found grace before thine eyes, shew unto thy servant, by whom thou doest visit thy creature.
text.KJV: Then said I, Lord, I beseech thee, if I have found favour in thy sight, shew thy servant by whom thou visitest thy creature.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘I implore you, O Lord, if I have found favour in your sight, show your servant through whom you will visit your creation.’

text.KJV: And he said unto me, In the beginning, when the earth was made, before the borders of the world stood, or ever the winds blew,
text.NRSV: He said to me, “At the beginning of the circle of the earth, before the portals of the world were in place, and before the assembled winds blew,”

text.DRB: and before the voices of thunders sounded, and before the flashings of lightnings shined, and before the foundations of paradise were confirmed,
text.KJV: Before it thundered and lightened, or ever the foundations of paradise were laid,
text.NRSV: and before the rumblings of thunder sounded, and before the flashes of lightning shone, and before the foundations of paradise were laid,

text.DRB: and before beautiful flowers were seen, and before the moved powers were established, and before the innumerable hosts of Angels were gathered,
text.KJV: Before the fair flowers were seen, or ever the moveable powers were established, before the innumerable multitude of angels were gathered together,
text.NRSV: and before the beautiful flowers were seen, and before the powers of movements] were established, and before the innumerable hosts of angels were gathered together,

text.DRB: and before the heights of the air were advanced, and before the firmaments were named, & before the chimneys were hot in Sion,
text.KJV: Or ever the heights of the air were lifted up, before the measures of the firmament were named, or ever the chimneys in Sion were hot,
text.NRSV: and before the heights of the air were lifted up, and before the measures of the firmaments were named, and before the footstool of Zion was established,

text.DRB: & before the present years were searched out, and before their inventions that now sin, were put away, and they signed that made faith their treasure:
text.KJV: And ere the present years were sought out, and or ever the inventions of them that now sin were turned, before they were sealed that have gathered faith for a treasure:
text.NRSV: and before the present years were reckoned and before the imaginations of those who now sin were estranged, and before those who stored up treasures of faith were sealed—

text.KJV: Then did I consider these things, and they all were made through me alone, and through none other: by me also they shall be ended, and by none other.
text.NRSV: “then I planned these things, and they were made through me and not through another; and the end shall come through me and not through another.”
text.DRB: then I thought, and they were made by me only, and not by any other: and the end by me, and not by any other.
And I answered, and said: What separation of times shall there be? And when shall the end of the former be, and the beginning of that which followeth?
text.KJV: Then answered I and said, What shall be the parting asunder of the times? or when shall be the end of the first, and the beginning of it that followeth?
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘What will be the dividing of the times? Or when will be the end of the first age and the beginning of the age that follows?’

text.DRB: And he said to me, from Abraham unto Isaac, when Jacob and Esau were born of him, the hand of Jacob held from the beginning the heel of Esau,
text.KJV: And he said unto me, From Abraham unto Isaac, when Jacob and Esau were born of him, Jacob’s hand held first the heel of Esau.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘From Abraham to Isaac,] because from him were born Jacob and Esau, for Jacob’s hand held Esau’s heel from the beginning.

text.DRB: for the end of this world is Esau, and the beginning of the next Jacob.
text.KJV: For Esau is the end of the world, and Jacob is the beginning of it that followeth.
text.NRSV: Now Esau is the end of this age, and Jacob is the beginning of the age that follows.

text.DRB: The hand of a man between the heel and the hand. Ask no other thing Esdras.
text.KJV: The hand of man is betwixt the heel and the hand: other question, Esdras, ask thou not.
text.NRSV: The beginning of a person is the hand, and the end of a person is the heel;] seek for nothing else, Ezra, between the heel and the hand, Ezra!’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: O Lord Dominator, if I have found grace before thine eyes,
text.KJV: I answered then and said, O Lord that bearest rule, if I have found favour in thy sight,
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘O sovereign Lord, if I have found favour in your sight,

text.DRB: I pray thee shew thy servant the end of thy signs, whereof thou didst shew me part the night before.
text.KJV: I beseech thee, shew thy servant the end of thy tokens, whereof thou shewedst me part the last night.
text.NRSV: show your servant the last of your signs of which you showed me a part on a previous night.’

text.DRB: And he answered, and said to me: Arise upon thy feet, and hear a voice most full of sound.
text.KJV: So he answered and said unto me, Stand up upon thy feet, and hear a mighty sounding voice.
text.NRSV: He answered and said to me, ‘Rise to your feet and you will hear a full, resounding voice.

text.DRB: And it shall be as it were a commotion, neither shall the place be moved wherein thou standest.
text.KJV: And it shall be as it were a great motion; but the place where thou standest shall not be moved.
text.NRSV: And if the place where you are standing is greatly shaken

text.DRB: Therefore when it speaketh be not thou afraid, because of the end is the word, and the foundation of the earth understood,
text.KJV: And therefore when it speaketh be not afraid: for the word is of the end, and the foundation of the earth is understood.
text.NRSV: while the voice is speaking, do not be terrified; because the word concerns the end, and the foundations of the earth will understand

text.DRB: for concerning them the word trembleth and is moved, for it knoweth that their end must be changed.
text.KJV: And why? because the speech of these things trembleth and is moved: for it knoweth that the end of these things must be changed.
text.NRSV: that the speech concerns them. They will tremble and be shaken, for they know that their end must be changed.’

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when I had heard, I rose upon my feet, and I heard: and behold a voice speaking, and the sound thereof as the sound of many waters:
text.KJV: And it happened, that when I had heard it I stood up upon my feet, and hearkened, and, behold, there was a voice that spake, and the sound of it was like the sound of many waters.
text.NRSV: When I heard this, I got to my feet and listened; a voice was speaking, and its sound was like the sound of mighty] waters.

text.DRB: and it said: Behold the days come, and the time shall be when I will begin to approach, that I may visit the inhabitants upon the earth.
text.KJV: And it said, Behold, the days come, that I will begin to draw nigh, and to visit them that dwell upon the earth,
text.NRSV: It said, ‘The days are coming when I draw near to visit the inhabitants of the earth,

text.DRB: And when I will begin to inquire of them that unjustly have hurt with their injustice, and when the humility of Sion shall be accomplished.
text.KJV: And will begin to make inquisition of them, what they be that have hurt unjustly with their unrighteousness, and when the affliction of Sion shall be fulfilled;
text.NRSV: and when I require from the doers of iniquity the penalty of their iniquity, and when the humiliation of Zion is complete.

text.KJV: And when the world, that shall begin to vanish away, shall be finished, then will I shew these tokens: the books shall be opened before the firmament, and they shall see all together:
text.NRSV: “And when the seal is placed upon the age that is about to pass away, then I will show these signs:”
text.DRB: And when the world shall be oversigned that shall begin to pass, I will do these signs: Books shall be opened before the face of the firmament, and all shall see together,
& infants of one year shall speak with their voices, and women with child shall bring forth untimely infants, not ripe, of three or four months, and shall live, and shall be raised up.
text.KJV: And the children of a year old shall speak with their voices, the women with child shall bring forth untimely children of three or four months old, and they shall live, and be raised up.
text.NRSV: Children a year old shall speak with their voices, and pregnant women shall give birth to premature children at three and four months, and these shall live and leap about.

text.DRB: And suddenly shall appear sown places not sown, and full cellars shall suddenly be found empty:
text.KJV: And suddenly shall the sown places appear unsown, the full storehouses shall suddenly be found empty:
text.NRSV: Sown places shall suddenly appear unsown, and full storehouses shall suddenly be found to be empty;

text.DRB: and a trumpet shall sound; which when all shall hear, they will suddenly be afraid.
text.KJV: And the trumpet shall give a sound, which when every man heareth, they shall be suddenly afraid.
text.NRSV: the trumpet shall sound aloud, and when all hear it, they shall suddenly be terrified.

text.DRB: And it shall be in that time, friends as enemies shall overthrow friends, and the earth shall be afraid with them: and the veins of fountains shall stand, and shall not run in three hours:
text.KJV: At that time shall friends fight one against another like enemies, and the earth shall stand in fear with those that dwell therein, the springs of the fountains shall stand still, and in three hours they shall not run.
text.NRSV: At that time friends shall make war on friends like enemies, the earth and those who inhabit it shall be terrified, and the springs of the fountains shall stand still, so that for three hours they shall not flow.

text.DRB: and it shall be, every one that shall be left of all these, of whom I have foretold thee, he shall be saved, and shall see my salvation, and the end of your world.
text.KJV: Whosoever remaineth from all these that I have told thee shall escape, and see my salvation, and the end of your world.
text.NRSV: ‘It shall be that whoever remains after all that I have foretold to you shall be saved and shall see my salvation and the end of my world.

text.DRB: And the men that are received, shall see, they that tasted not death from their nativity, and the heart of the inhabitants shall be turned into another sense.
text.KJV: And the men that are received shall see it, who have not tasted death from their birth: and the heart of the inhabitants shall be changed, and turned into another meaning.
text.NRSV: And they shall see those who were taken up, who from their birth have not tasted death; and the heart of the earth’s] inhabitants shall be changed and converted to a different spirit.

text.DRB: For evil shall be put out, and deceit shall be extinguished,
text.KJV: For evil shall be put out, and deceit shall be quenched.
text.NRSV: For evil shall be blotted out, and deceit shall be quenched;

text.DRB: but faith shall flourish, and corruption shall be overcome, and truth shall be shewed, which was without fruit so many days.
text.KJV: As for faith, it shall flourish, corruption shall be overcome, and the truth, which hath been so long without fruit, shall be declared.
text.NRSV: faithfulness shall flourish, and corruption shall be overcome, and the truth, which has been so long without fruit, shall be revealed.’

text.DRB: And it came to pass, when he spake to me, and I lo by little and little looked on him before whom I stood,
text.KJV: And when he talked with me, behold, I looked by little and little upon him before whom I stood.
text.NRSV: While he spoke to me, little by little the place where I was standing began to rock to and fro.]

text.DRB: and he said to me these words: I am come to shew thee the time of the night to come.
text.KJV: And these words said he unto me; I am come to shew thee the time of the night to come.
text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘I have come to show you these things this night.]

text.DRB: If therefore thou pray again, and fast again seven days, again I will tell thee greater things by the day which I have heard.
text.KJV: If thou wilt pray yet more, and fast seven days again, I shall tell thee greater things by day than I have heard.
text.NRSV: If therefore you will pray again and fast again for seven days, I will again declare to you greater things than these,]

text.DRB: For thy voice is heard before the Highest. For the strong hath seen thy direction, and hath foreseen the chastity which thou hast had from thy youth:
text.KJV: For thy voice is heard before the most High: for the Mighty hath seen thy righteous dealing, he hath seen also thy chastity, which thou hast had ever since thy youth.
text.NRSV: because your voice has surely been heard by the Most High; for the Mighty One has seen your uprightness and has also observed the purity that you have maintained from your youth.

text.DRB: and for this cause he hath sent me to shew thee all these
text.KJV: And therefore hath he sent me to shew thee all these things, and to say unto thee, Be of good comfort and fear not
text.NRSV: Therefore he sent me to show you all these things, and to say to you: “Believe and do not be afraid!

text.DRB: and hasten not with the former times to think vain things, that thou hasten not from the last times.
text.KJV: And hasten not with the times that are past, to think vain things, that thou mayest not hasten from the latter times.
text.NRSV: Do not be quick to think vain thoughts concerning the former times; then you will not act hastily in the last times.”’

text.DRB: And it came to pass after these things, and I wept again, and in like manner I fasted seven days, to accomplish the three weeks, that were told me.
text.KJV: And it came to pass after this, that I wept again, and fasted seven days in like manner, that I might fulfil the three weeks which he told me.
text.NRSV: Now after this I wept again and fasted for seven days in the same way as before, in order to complete the three weeks that had been prescribed for me.

text.DRB: And it came to pass in the eighth night, and my heart was troubled again in me, and I began to speak before the Highest.
text.KJV: And in the eighth night was my heart vexed within me again, and I began to speak before the most High.
text.NRSV: Then on the eighth night my heart was troubled within me again, and I began to speak in the presence of the Most High.

text.DRB: For my spirit was inflamed exceedingly, and my soul was distressed.
text.KJV: For my spirit was greatly set on fire, and my soul was in distress.
text.NRSV: My spirit was greatly aroused, and my soul was in distress.

text.DRB: And I said: O Lord, speaking thou didst speak from the beginning of creature from the first day, saying: Let heaven be made and earth: and thy word was a perfect work.
text.KJV: And I said, O Lord, thou spakest from the beginning of the creation, even the first day, and saidst thus; Let heaven and earth be made; and thy word was a perfect work.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘O Lord, you spoke at the beginning of creation, and said on the first day, “Let heaven and earth be made”, and your word accomplished the work.

text.DRB: And then there was spirit, and darkness was carried about, and silence, the sound of the voice of man was not yet from thee.
text.KJV: And then was the spirit, and darkness and silence were on every side; the sound of man’s voice was not yet formed.
text.NRSV: Then the spirit was blowing, and darkness and silence embraced everything; the sound of human voices was not yet there.]

text.DRB: Then thou didst command the lightsome light to be brought forth of thy treasures, whereby thy work might appear.
text.KJV: Then commandedst thou a fair light to come forth of thy treasures, that thy work might appear.
text.NRSV: Then you commanded a ray of light to be brought out from your store-chambers, so that your works could be seen.

text.DRB: And in the second day thou didst create the spirit of the firmament, and commandest it to divide, and to make a division between the waters, that a certain part should depart upward, & part should remain beneath.
text.KJV: Upon the second day thou madest the spirit of the firmament, and commandedst it to part asunder, and to make a division betwixt the waters, that the one part might go up, and the other remain beneath.
text.NRSV: ‘Again, on the second day, you created the spirit of the firmament, and commanded it to divide and separate the waters, so that one part might move upwards and the other part remain beneath.

text.DRB: And in the third day thou didst command the waters to be gathered together in the seventh part of the earth: but six parts thou didst dry and preserve, that of them might be serving before thee things sown of God, and tilled.
text.KJV: Upon the third day thou didst command that the waters should be gathered in the seventh part of the earth: six pats hast thou dried up, and kept them, to the intent that of these some being planted of God and tilled might serve thee.
text.NRSV: ‘On the third day you commanded the waters to be gathered together in a seventh part of the earth; six parts you dried up and kept so that some of them might be planted and cultivated and be of service before you.

text.DRB: For thy word proceeded, and the work forthwith was made.
text.KJV: For as soon as thy word went forth the work was made.
text.NRSV: For your word went forth, and at once the work was done.

text.DRB: For suddenly came forth fruit of multitude infinite, and diverse tastes of concupiscence, and flowers of unchangeable colour, and odours of unsearchable smell, & in the third day these things were made.
text.KJV: For immediately there was great and innumerable fruit, and many and divers pleasures for the taste, and flowers of unchangeable colour, and odours of wonderful smell: and this was done the third day.
text.NRSV: Immediately fruit came forth in endless abundance and of varied appeal to the taste, and flowers of inimitable colour, and odours of inexpressible fragrance. These were made on the third day.

text.DRB: And in the fourth day thou didst command to be made the brightness of the sun, the light of the moon, the disposition of the stars:
text.KJV: Upon the fourth day thou commandedst that the sun should shine, and the moon give her light, and the stars should be in order:
text.NRSV: ‘On the fourth day you commanded the brightness of the sun, the light of the moon, and the arrangement of the stars to come into being;

text.DRB: and didst command them that they should serve man, that should be made.
text.KJV: And gavest them a charge to do service unto man, that was to be made.
text.NRSV: and you commanded them to serve humankind, about to be formed.

text.DRB: And in the fifth day thou saidst to the seventh part, where the water was gathered together, that it should bring forth beasts, & fowls & fishes: & so was it done,
text.KJV: Upon the fifth day thou saidst unto the seventh part, where the waters were gathered that it should bring forth living creatures, fowls and fishes: and so it came to pass.
text.NRSV: ‘On the fifth day you commanded the seventh part, where the water had been gathered together, to bring forth living creatures, birds, and fishes; and so it was done.

text.DRB: the dumb water and without life, the things that by God's appointment were commanded, made beasts, that thereby the nations may declare thy marvelous works.
text.KJV: For the dumb water and without life brought forth living things at the commandment of God, that all people might praise thy wondrous works.
text.NRSV: The dumb and lifeless water produced living creatures, as it was commanded, so that therefore the nations might declare your wondrous works.

text.DRB: And then thou didst preserve two souls: the name of one thou didst call Henoch, and the name of the second thou didst call Leviathan,
text.KJV: Then didst thou ordain two living creatures, the one thou calledst Enoch, and the other Leviathan;
text.NRSV: ‘Then you kept in existence two living creatures;] one you called Behemoth] and the name of the other was Leviathan.

text.DRB: and thou didst separate them from each other. For the seventh part, where the water was gathered together, could not hold them.
text.KJV: And didst separate the one from the other: for the seventh part, namely, where the water was gathered together, might not hold them both.
text.NRSV: And you separated one from the other, for the seventh part where the water had been gathered together could not hold them both.

text.DRB: And thou gavest to Henoch one part, which was dried the third day, to dwell therein, where are a thousand mountains.
text.KJV: Unto Enoch thou gavest one part, which was dried up the third day, that he should dwell in the same part, wherein are a thousand hills:
text.NRSV: And you gave Behemoth] one of the parts that had been dried up on the third day, to live in it, where there are a thousand mountains;

text.DRB: But to Leviathan thou gavest the seventh part being moist, and kepst it, that it might be to devour whom thou wilt, and when thou wilt.
text.KJV: But unto Leviathan thou gavest the seventh part, namely, the moist; and hast kept him to be devoured of whom thou wilt, and when.
text.NRSV: but to Leviathan you gave the seventh part, the watery part; and you have kept them to be eaten by whom you wish, and when you wish.

text.DRB: And in the sixth day thou didst command the earth to create before thee cattle, and beasts, and creeping creatures:
text.KJV: Upon the sixth day thou gavest commandment unto the earth, that before thee it should bring forth beasts, cattle, and creeping things:
text.NRSV: ‘On the sixth day you commanded the earth to bring forth before you cattle, wild animals, and creeping things;

text.KJV: And after these, Adam also, whom thou madest lord of all thy creatures: of him come we all, and the people also whom thou hast chosen.
text.NRSV: “And after these, Adam, whom you made lord over all the creatures which you had made; and from him we have all come, the people whom you have chosen.”
text.DRB: and over these Adam, whom thou madest ruler over all the works, which thou didst make, and out of him are all we brought forth, and the people whom thou hast chosen.
And all these things I have said before thee o Lord, because thou didst create the world for us.
text.KJV: All this have I spoken before thee, O Lord, because thou madest the world for our sakes
text.NRSV: ‘All this I have spoken before you, O Lord, because you have said that it was for us that you created this world.]

text.DRB: But the residue of the nations born of Adam thou saidst that they were nothing, and that they were like to spittle, and as it were the dropping out of a vessel thou didst liken the aboundance of them.
text.KJV: As for the other people, which also come of Adam, thou hast said that they are nothing, but be like unto spittle: and hast likened the abundance of them unto a drop that falleth from a vessel.
text.NRSV: As for the other nations that have descended from Adam, you have said that they are nothing, and that they are like spittle, and you have compared their abundance to a drop from a bucket.

text.DRB: And now Lord, behold these nations which are reputed for nothing, have begun to rule over us, and to devour us:
text.KJV: And now, O Lord, behold, these heathen, which have ever been reputed as nothing, have begun to be lords over us, and to devour us.
text.NRSV: And now, O Lord, these nations, which are reputed to be as nothing, domineer over us and devour us.

text.DRB: but we thy people whom thou didst call thy first only begotten emulator, are delivered into their hands:
text.KJV: But we thy people, whom thou hast called thy firstborn, thy only begotten, and thy fervent lover, are given into their hands.
text.NRSV: But we your people, whom you have called your firstborn, only-begotten, zealous for you,] and most dear, have been given into their hands.

text.DRB: and if the world was created for us, why do not we possess inheritance with the world? How long these things?
text.KJV: If the world now be made for our sakes, why do we not possess an inheritance with the world? how long shall this endure?
text.NRSV: If the world has indeed been created for us, why do we not possess our world as an inheritance? How long will this be so?’

text.KJV: And when I had made an end of speaking these words, there was sent unto me the angel which had been sent unto me the nights afore:
text.NRSV: When I had finished speaking these words, the angel who had been sent to me on the former nights was sent to me again.
text.DRB: Jesus shall be revealed with them that are with him, & they shall be merry that are left in the four hundred years.
and he said to me: Arise Esdras, and hear the words which I am come to speak to thee.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Up, Esdras, and hear the words that I am come to tell thee.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Rise, Ezra, and listen to the words that I have come to speak to you.’

text.DRB: And I said: Speak my God. And he said to me: The sea is set in a large place, that it might be deep and wide:
text.KJV: And I said, Speak on, my God. Then said he unto me, The sea is set in a wide place, that it might be deep and great.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘Speak, my lord.’ And he said to me, ‘There is a sea set in a wide expanse so that it is deep and vast,

text.DRB: but the entrance to it shall be set in a strait place, that it might be like to rivers.
text.KJV: But put the case the entrance were narrow, and like a river;
text.NRSV: but it has an entrance set in a narrow place, so that it is like a river.

text.DRB: For who witting will enter into the sea, and see it, or rule over it: if he pass not the strait, how shall he come into the breadth?
text.KJV: Who then could go into the sea to look upon it, and to rule it? if he went not through the narrow, how could he come into the broad?
text.NRSV: If there are those who wish to reach the sea, to look at it or to navigate it, how can they come to the broad part unless they pass through the narrow part?

text.DRB: Also another thing: A city is built, and set in a plain place, and it is full of all goods.
text.KJV: There is also another thing; A city is builded, and set upon a broad field, and is full of all good things:
text.NRSV: Another example: There is a city built and set on a plain, and it is full of all good things;

text.DRB: And it came to pass when I had ended to speak these words, the Angel was sent to me, which had been sent to me the first nights,
text.KJV: The entrance thereof is narrow, and is set in a dangerous place to fall, like as if there were a fire on the right hand, and on the left a deep water:
text.NRSV: but the entrance to it is narrow and set in a precipitous place, so that there is fire on the right hand and deep water on the left.

text.DRB: and there is one only path set between them, that is, between the fire and the water, so that the path cannot contain, but only a man's step.
text.KJV: And one only path between them both, even between the fire and the water, so small that there could but one man go there at once.
text.NRSV: There is only one path lying between them, that is, between the fire and the water, so that only one person can walk on the path.

text.DRB: And if the city shall be given a man for inheritance, if he never pass through the peril set before it, how shall he receive his inheritance?
text.KJV: If this city now were given unto a man for an inheritance, if he never shall pass the danger set before it, how shall he receive this inheritance?
text.NRSV: If now the city is given to someone as an inheritance, how will the heir receive the inheritance unless by passing through the appointed danger?’

text.DRB: And I said: So Lord. And he said to me, So it is: Israel also a part.
text.KJV: And I said, It is so, Lord. Then said he unto me, Even so also is Israel’s portion.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘That is right, lord.’ He said to me, ‘So also is Israel’s portion.

text.KJV: Because for their sakes I made the world: and when Adam transgressed my statutes, then was decreed that now is done.
text.NRSV: “For I made the world for their sake, and when Adam transgressed my statutes, what had been made was judged.”
text.DRB: For I made the world for them: and when Adam transgressed my constitution, that was judged which was done.
And the entrances of this world were made strait, and sorrowful, & painful, and few and evil, and full of dangers, and stuffed very much with labour.
text.KJV: Then were the entrances of this world made narrow, full of sorrow and travail: they are but few and evil, full of perils,: and very painful.
text.NRSV: And so the entrances of this world were made narrow and sorrowful and toilsome; they are few and evil, full of dangers and involved in great hardships.

text.DRB: For the entrances of the greater world are large and secure, and making fruit of immortality.
text.KJV: For the entrances of the elder world were wide and sure, and brought immortal fruit.
text.NRSV: But the entrances of the greater world are broad and safe, and yield the fruit of immortality.

text.DRB: If then they that live entering in enter into these strait and vain things: they cannot receive the things that are laid up.
text.KJV: If then they that live labour not to enter these strait and vain things, they can never receive those that are laid up for them.
text.NRSV: Therefore unless the living pass through the difficult and futile experiences, they can never receive those things that have been reserved for them.

text.DRB: Now therefore why art thou troubled, whereas thou art corruptible? And why art thou moved, whereas thou art mortal?
text.KJV: Now therefore why disquietest thou thyself, seeing thou art but a corruptible man? and why art thou moved, whereas thou art but mortal?
text.NRSV: Now therefore, why are you disturbed, seeing that you are to perish? Why are you moved, seeing that you are mortal?

text.DRB: And why hast thou not taken in thy heart that which is to come, but that which is present?
text.KJV: Why hast thou not considered in thy mind this thing that is to come, rather than that which is present?
text.NRSV: Why have you not considered in your mind what is to come, rather than what is now present?’

text.DRB: I answered, and said: Lord Dominator: behold thou hast disposed by thy law that the just shall inherit these things, and the impious shall perish. .
text.KJV: Then answered I and said, O Lord that bearest rule, thou hast ordained in thy law, that the righteous should inherit these things, but that the ungodly should perish.
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘O sovereign Lord, you have ordained in your law that the righteous shall inherit these things, but that the ungodly shall perish.

text.DRB: But the just shall suffer the straits, hoping for the wide places: for they that have done impiously, have both suffered the straits, and shall not see the wide places.
text.KJV: Nevertheless the righteous shall suffer strait things, and hope for wide: for they that have done wickedly have suffered the strait things, and yet shall not see the wide.
text.NRSV: The righteous, therefore, can endure difficult circumstances while hoping for easier ones; but those who have done wickedly have suffered the difficult circumstances and will never see the easier ones.’

text.DRB: And he said to me: There is no judge above God, nor that understandeth above the Highest.
text.KJV: And he said unto me. There is no judge above God, and none that hath understanding above the Highest.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘You are not a better judge than the Lord,] or wiser than the Most High!

text.DRB: For many present do perish because the law of God which was set before, is neglected.
text.KJV: For there be many that perish in this life, because they despise the law of God that is set before them.
text.NRSV: Let many perish who are now living, rather than that the law of God that is set before them be disregarded!

text.DRB: For God commanding commanded them that came, when they came, what doing they should live, and what observing they should not be punished.
text.KJV: For God hath given strait commandment to such as came, what they should do to live, even as they came, and what they should observe to avoid punishment.
text.NRSV: For the Lord] strictly commanded those who came into the world, when they came, what they should do to live, and what they should observe to avoid punishment.

text.DRB: But they were not persuaded, & gainsaid him, and made to themselves a cogitation of vanity,
text.KJV: Nevertheless they were not obedient unto him; but spake against him, and imagined vain things;
text.NRSV: they devised for themselves vain thoughts,

text.DRB: and proposed to themselves deceits of sins, and they said to the Highest that he was not, and they knew not his ways,
text.KJV: And deceived themselves by their wicked deeds; and said of the most High, that he is not; and knew not his ways:
text.NRSV: and they ignored his ways.

text.DRB: and despised his law, and denied his covenances, and had not fidelity in his ordinances, & did not accomplish his works.
text.KJV: But his law have they despised, and denied his covenants; in his statutes have they not been faithful, and have not performed his works.
text.NRSV: and have not performed his works.

text.DRB: For this cause Esdras, the empty to the empty, & the full to the full.
text.KJV: And therefore, Esdras, for the empty are empty things, and for the full are the full things.
text.NRSV: That is the reason, Ezra, that empty things are for the empty, and full things are for the full.

text.DRB: Behold the time shall come, & it shall be when the signs shall come which I have foretold thee, and the bride shall appear, and appearing she shall be shewed that now is hid with the earth:
text.KJV: Behold, the time shall come, that these tokens which I have told thee shall come to pass, and the bride shall appear, and she coming forth shall be seen, that now is withdrawn from the earth.
text.NRSV: ‘For indeed the time will come, when the signs that I have foretold to you will come to pass, that the city that now is not seen shall appear,] and the land that now is hidden shall be disclosed.

text.DRB: and every one that is delivered from the foresaid evils, he shall see my marvelous things.
text.KJV: And whosoever is delivered from the foresaid evils shall see my wonders.
text.NRSV: Everyone who has been delivered from the evils that I have foretold shall see my wonders.

text.KJV: For my son Jesus shall be revealed with those that be with him, and they that remain shall rejoice within four hundred years.
text.NRSV: “For my son the Messiah shall be revealed with those who are with him, and those who remain shall rejoice four hundred years.”
text.DRB: For my son JESUS shall be revealed with them that are with him, & they shall be merry that are left in the four hundred years.

text.KJV: After these years shall my son Christ die, and all men that have life.
text.NRSV: “After those years my son the Messiah shall die, and all who draw human breath.”
text.DRB: And it shall be after these years, and my son CHRIST shall die: and all men that have breadth,

text.KJV: And the world shall be turned into the old silence seven days, like as in the former judgments: so that no man shall remain.
text.NRSV: “Then the world shall be turned back to primeval silence for seven days, as it was at the first beginnings, so that no one shall be left.”
text.DRB: and the world shall be turned into the old silence seven days, as in the former judgements, so that none shall be left.
And it shall be after seven days and the world shall be raised up that yet waketh not, and shall die corrupted:
text.KJV: And after seven days the world, that yet awaketh not, shall be raised up, and that shall die that is corrupt
text.NRSV: After seven days the world that is not yet awake shall be roused, and that which is corruptible shall perish.

text.KJV: And the earth shall restore those that are asleep in her, and so shall the dust those that dwell in silence, and the secret places shall deliver those souls that were committed unto them.
text.NRSV: “The earth shall give up those who are asleep in it, and the dust those who rest there in silence; and the chambers shall give up the souls which have been committed to them.”
text.DRB: and the earth shall render the things that sleep in it, and the dust them that dwell in it with silence, and the cellars shall render the souls that are commended to them.
And the Highest shall be revealed upon the seat of judgement, and miseries shall pass, and long sufferance shall be gathered together.
text.KJV: And the most High shall appear upon the seat of judgment, and misery shall pass away, and the long suffering shall have an end:
text.NRSV: The Most High shall be revealed on the seat of judgement, and compassion shall pass away, and patience shall be withdrawn.]

text.DRB: And judgement only shall remain, truth shall stand, and faith shall wax strong,
text.KJV: But judgment only shall remain, truth shall stand, and faith shall wax strong:
text.NRSV: Only judgement shall remain, truth shall stand, and faithfulness shall grow strong.

text.DRB: and the work shall follow, and the reward shall be shewed, and justice shall awake, and injustice shall not have dominion.
text.KJV: And the work shall follow, and the reward shall be shewed, and the good deeds shall be of force, and wicked deeds shall bear no rule.
text.NRSV: Recompense shall follow, and the reward shall be manifested; righteous deeds shall awake, and unrighteous deeds shall not sleep.]

text.DRB: And I said: First Abraham prayed for the Sodomites, and Moyses for the fathers that sinned in the desert. . Exod. 32.
text.KJV: Then said I, Abraham prayed first for the Sodomites, and Moses for the fathers that sinned in the wilderness:
text.NRSV: The pit] of torment shall appear, and opposite it shall be the place of rest; and the furnace of hell] shall be disclosed, and opposite it the paradise of delight.

text.DRB: And they that were after him for Israel in the
text.KJV: And Jesus after him for Israel in the time of Achan:
text.NRSV: Then the Most High will say to the nations that have been raised from the dead, “Look now, and understand whom you have denied, whom you have not served, whose commandments you have despised.

text.DRB: And David for the destruction, and of Salomon for them that came unto the sanctification.
text.KJV: And Samuel and David for the destruction: and Solomon for them that should come to the sanctuary:
text.NRSV: Look on this side and on that; here are delight and rest, and there are fire and torments.” Thus he will] speak to them on the day of judgement—

text.DRB: And Elias for them that received rain, and for the dead that he might live, 3. . & 18.
text.KJV: And Helias for those that received rain; and for the dead, that he might live:
text.NRSV: a day that has no sun or moon or stars,

text.DRB: and Ezechias for the people in the days of Sennacherib, and many for many.
text.KJV: And Ezechias for the people in the time of Sennacherib: and many for many.
text.NRSV: or cloud or thunder or lightning, or wind or water or air, or darkness or evening or morning,

text.DRB: If therefore now when corruptible did increase, and injustice was multiplied, and the just prayed for the impious: why now also shall it not be so?
text.KJV: Even so now, seeing corruption is grown up, and wickedness increased, and the righteous have prayed for the ungodly: wherefore shall it not be so now also?
text.NRSV: or summer or spring or heat or winter] or frost or cold, or hail or rain or dew,

text.DRB: And he answered me and said: This present world is not the end, much glory remaineth in it: for this cause they prayed for the impotent.
text.KJV: He answered me, and said, This present life is not the end where much glory doth abide; therefore have they prayed for the weak.
text.NRSV: or noon or night, or dawn or shining or brightness or light, but only the splendour of the glory of the Most High, by which all shall see what has been destined.

text.DRB: For the day of judgement shall be the end of this time, and the beginning of the immortality to come, wherein corruption is past:
text.KJV: But the day of doom shall be the end of this time, and the beginning of the immortality for to come, wherein corruption is past,
text.NRSV: It will last as though for a week of years.

text.DRB: intemperance is dissolved, incredulity is cut off: and justice hath increased, truth is sprung.
text.KJV: Intemperance is at an end, infidelity is cut off, righteousness is grown, and truth is sprung up.
text.NRSV: This is my judgement and its prescribed order; and to you alone I have shown these things.’

text.DRB: For then no man can save him that hath perished, nor drown him that hath overcome. And I answered,
text.KJV: Then shall no man be able to save him that is destroyed, nor to oppress him that hath gotten the victory.
text.NRSV: I answered and said, ‘O sovereign Lord, I said then and] I say now: Blessed are those who are alive and keep your commandments!

text.DRB: and said: This is my word the first and the last, that it had been better not to give the earth to Adam, or when he had now given it, to restrain him that he should not sin.
text.KJV: I answered then and said, This is my first and last saying, that it had been better not to have given the earth unto Adam: or else, when it was given him, to have restrained him from sinning.
text.NRSV: But what of those for whom I prayed? For who among the living is there that has not sinned, or who is there among mortals that has not transgressed your covenant?

text.DRB: For what doth it profit men presently to live in sorrow, and being dead to hope for punishment?
text.KJV: For what profit is it for men now in this present time to live in heaviness, and after death to look for punishment?
text.NRSV: And now I see that the world to come will bring delight to few, but torments to many.

text.DRB: O what hast thou done Adam? For if thou didst sin, it was not made thy fall only, but ours also which came of thee.
text.KJV: O thou Adam, what hast thou done? for though it was thou that sinned, thou art not fallen alone, but we all that come of thee.
text.NRSV: For an evil heart has grown up in us, which has alienated us from God,] and has brought us into corruption and the ways of death, and has shown us the paths of perdition and removed us far from life—and that not merely for a few but for almost all who have been created.’

text.DRB: For what doth it profit us if immortal time be promised to us: but we have done mortal works?
text.KJV: For what profit is it unto us, if there be promised us an immortal time, whereas we have done the works that bring death?
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Listen to me, Ezra,] and I will instruct you, and will admonish you once more.

text.DRB: And that everlasting hope is foretold us: but we most wicked are become vain?
text.KJV: And that there is promised us an everlasting hope, whereas ourselves being most wicked are made vain?
text.NRSV: For this reason the Most High has made not one world but two.

text.DRB: And that habitations of health and security are reserved for us, but we have converst naughtily?
text.KJV: And that there are laid up for us dwellings of health and safety, whereas we have lived wickedly?
text.NRSV: Inasmuch as you have said that the righteous are not many but few, while the ungodly abound, hear the explanation for this.

text.DRB: And that the glory of the Highest is reserved to protect them that have slowly converst: but we have walked in most wicked ways?
text.KJV: And that the glory of the most High is kept to defend them which have led a wary life, whereas we have walked in the most wicked ways of all?
text.NRSV: ‘If you have just a few precious stones, will you add to them lead and clay?’]

text.DRB: And that paradise shall be shewed, whose fruit continueth incorrupted, wherein is security and remedy:
text.KJV: And that there should be shewed a paradise, whose fruit endureth for ever, wherein is security and medicine, since we shall not enter into it?
text.NRSV: I said, ‘Lord, how could that be?’

text.DRB: but we shall not enter in: for we have converst in unlawful places?
text.KJV: (For we have walked in unpleasant places.)
text.NRSV: And he said to me, ‘Not only that, but ask the earth and she will tell you; defer to her, and she will declare it to you.

text.DRB: And their faces which have had abstinence, shall shine above the stars: but our faces black above darkness.
text.KJV: And that the faces of them which have used abstinence shall shine above the stars, whereas our faces shall be blacker than darkness?
text.NRSV: Say to her, “You produce gold and silver and bronze, and also iron and lead and clay;

text.DRB: For we did not think living when we did iniquity, that we shall begin after death to suffer.
text.KJV: For while we lived and committed iniquity, we considered not that we should begin to suffer for it after death.
text.NRSV: but silver is more abundant than gold, and bronze than silver, and iron than bronze, and lead than iron, and clay than lead.”

text.DRB: And he answered, & said: This is the cogitation of the battle which man shall fight, who is born upon the earth,
text.KJV: Then answered he me, and said, This is the condition of the battle, which man that is born upon the earth shall fight;
text.NRSV: Judge therefore which things are precious and desirable, those that are abundant or those that are rare?’

text.DRB: that if he shall be overcome, he suffer that which thou hast said: but if he overcome he shall receive that which I say:
text.KJV: That, if he be overcome, he shall suffer as thou hast said: but if he get the victory, he shall receive the thing that I say.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘O sovereign Lord, what is plentiful is of less worth, for what is more rare is more precious.’

text.DRB: for this is the life which Moyses spake of when he lived, to the people, saying: Choose unto thee life, that thou mayst live.
text.KJV: For this is the life whereof Moses spake unto the people while he lived, saying, Choose thee life, that thou mayest live.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Consider within yourself] what you have thought, for the person who has what is hard to get rejoices more than the person who has what is plentiful.

text.DRB: But they believed him not, no nor the Prophets after him, no nor me which have spoken to them.
text.KJV: Nevertheless they believed not him, nor yet the prophets after him, no nor me which have spoken unto them,
text.NRSV: So also will be the judgement] that I have promised; for I will rejoice over the few who shall be saved, because it is they who have made my glory prevail now, and through them my name has now been honoured.

text.DRB: Because there should not be sorrow unto their perdition, as there shall be joy upon them, to whom salvation is persuaded.
text.KJV: That there should not be such heaviness in their destruction, as shall be joy over them that are persuaded to salvation.
text.NRSV: I will not grieve over the great number of those who perish; for it is they who are now like a mist, and are similar to a flame and smoke—they are set on fire and burn hotly, and are extinguished.’

text.DRB: And I answered, and said: I know Lord, that the Highest is called merciful in that, he hath mercy on them which are not yet come into the world,
text.KJV: I answered then, and said, I know, Lord, that the most High is called merciful, in that he hath mercy upon them which are not yet come into the world,
text.NRSV: I replied and said, ‘O earth, what have you brought forth, if the mind is made out of the dust like the other created things?

text.DRB: and that he hath mercy on them which converse in his law:
text.KJV: And upon those also that turn to his law;
text.NRSV: For it would have been better if the dust itself had not been born, so that the mind might not have been made from it.

text.DRB: & he is long suffering, because he sheweth long sufferance to them that have sinned, as it were with their own works:
text.KJV: And that he is patient, and long suffereth those that have sinned, as his creatures;
text.NRSV: But now the mind grows with us, and therefore we are tormented, because we perish and we know it.

text.DRB: and he is bountiful, because he will give according to exigents:
text.KJV: And that he is bountiful, for he is ready to give where it needeth;
text.NRSV: Let the human race lament, but let the wild animals of the field be glad; let all who have been born lament, but let the cattle and the flocks rejoice.

text.DRB: and of great mercy, because he multiplieth more mercies to them that are present, and that are past, and that are to come.
text.KJV: And that he is of great mercy, for he multiplieth more and more mercies to them that are present, and that are past, and also to them which are to come.
text.NRSV: It is much better with them than with us; for they do not look for a judgement, and they do not know of any torment or salvation promised to them after death.

text.DRB: For if he shall not multiply his mercies, the world shall not be made alive with them that did inherit it.
text.KJV: For if he shall not multiply his mercies, the world would not continue with them that inherit therein.
text.NRSV: What does it profit us that we shall be preserved alive but cruelly tormented?

text.DRB: And he giveth: for if he shall not give of his bounty, that they may be relieved which have done iniquity, the tenth thousand part of men cannot be quickened from their iniquities.
text.KJV: And he pardoneth; for if he did not so of his goodness, that they which have committed iniquities might be eased of them, the ten thousandth part of men should not remain living.
text.NRSV: For all who have been born are entangled in] iniquities, and are full of sins and burdened with transgressions.

text.DRB: And the judge if he shall not forgive them that are cured with his word, and wipe away a multitude of contentions: there should not perhaps be left in an innumerable multitude, but very few.
text.KJV: And being judge, if he should not forgive them that are cured with his word, and put out the multitude of contentions,
text.NRSV: And if after death we were not to come into judgement, perhaps it would have been better for us.’

text.KJV: There should be very few left peradventure in an innumerable multitude.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, “When the Most High made the world and Adam and all who have come from him, he first prepared the judgment and the things that belong to the judgment.”

text.KJV: And he answered me, saying, The most High hath made this world for many, but the world to come for few.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, “The Most High made this world for the sake of many, but the world to come for the sake of few.”

text.DRB: And I will speak a similitude Esdras before thee. For as thou shalt ask the earth, and it will tell thee, that it will give much more earth whereof earthen work may be made, but a little dust whereof gold is made: so also is the act of this present world.
text.KJV: I will tell thee a similitude, Esdras; As when thou askest the earth, it shall say unto thee, that it giveth much mould whereof earthen vessels are made, but little dust that gold cometh of: even so is the course of this present world.
text.NRSV: But I tell you a parable, Ezra. Just as, when you ask the earth, it will tell you that it provides a large amount of clay from which earthenware is made, but only a little dust from which gold comes, so is the course of the present world.

text.KJV: There be many created, but few shall be saved.
text.NRSV: “Many have been created, but few shall be saved.”
text.DRB: Many indeed are created, but few shall be saved.

text.KJV: So answered I and said, Swallow then down, O my soul, understanding, and devour wisdom.
text.NRSV: I answered and said, “Then drink your fill of understanding, O my soul, and drink wisdom, O my heart!”
text.DRB: And I answered, and said: Then o soul swallow up the sense, and devour that which is wise.
For thou art agreed to obey, and willing to prophesy. For there is no space given thee but only to live.
text.KJV: For thou hast agreed to give ear, and art willing to prophesy: for thou hast no longer space than only to live.
text.NRSV: For not of your own will did you come into the world,] and against your will you depart, for you have been given only a short time to live.

text.DRB: O Lord if thou wilt not permit thy servant, that we pray before thee, and thou give us seed to the heart, and tillage to the understanding, whereof may the fruit be made, whereby every corrupt person may live, that shall bear the place of a man?
text.KJV: O Lord, if thou suffer not thy servant, that we may pray before thee, and thou give us seed unto our heart, and culture to our understanding, that there may come fruit of it; how shall each man live that is corrupt, who beareth the place of a man?
text.NRSV: O Lord above us, grant to your servant that we may pray before you, and give us a seed for our heart and cultivation of our understanding so that fruit may be produced, by which every mortal who bears the likeness] of a human being may be able to live.

text.DRB: For thou art alone, and we are one workmanship of thy hands, as thou hast spoken:
text.KJV: For thou art alone, and we all one workmanship of thine hands, like as thou hast said.
text.NRSV: For you alone exist, and we are a work of your hands, as you have declared.

text.DRB: and as now the body made in the matrice, and thou doest give the members, thy creature is preserved in fire and water: and nine months thy workmanship doth suffer thy creature that is created in it:
text.KJV: For when the body is fashioned now in the mother’s womb, and thou givest it members, thy creature is preserved in fire and water, and nine months doth thy workmanship endure thy creature which is created in her.
text.NRSV: And because you give life to the body that is now fashioned in the womb, and furnish it with members, what you have created is preserved amid fire and water, and for nine months the womb] endures your creature that has been created in it.

text.DRB: and itself that keepeth, and that which is kept, both shall be preserved: & the matrice being preserved rendereth again at some time the things that are grown in it.
text.KJV: But that which keepeth and is kept shall both be preserved: and when the time cometh, the womb preserved delivereth up the things that grew in it.
text.NRSV: But that which keeps and that which is kept shall both be kept by your keeping.] And when the womb gives up again what has been created in it,

text.DRB: For thou hast commanded of the members, that is the breasts to give milk unto the fruit of the breasts,
text.KJV: For thou hast commanded out of the parts of the body, that is to say, out of the breasts, milk to be given, which is the fruit of the breasts,
text.NRSV: you have commanded that from the members themselves (that is, from the breasts) milk, the fruit of the breasts, should be supplied,

text.DRB: that the thing which is made, may be nourished till a certain time, and afterward thou mayest dispose him to thy mercy.
text.KJV: That the thing which is fashioned may be nourished for a time, till thou disposest it to thy mercy.
text.NRSV: so that what has been fashioned may be nourished for a time; and afterwards you will still guide it in your mercy.

text.DRB: For thou hast, brought him up in thy justice, and hast instructed him in thy law, and hast corrected him in thy understanding:
text.KJV: Thou broughtest it up with thy righteousness, and nurturedst it in thy law, and reformedst it with thy judgment.
text.NRSV: You have nurtured it in your righteousness, and instructed it in your law, and reproved it in your wisdom.

text.DRB: and thou shalt mortify him, as thy creature: and shalt give him life, as thy work.
text.KJV: And thou shalt mortify it as thy creature, and quicken it as thy work.
text.NRSV: You put it to death as your creation, and make it live as your work.

text.DRB: If then thou wilt destroy him that is made with so great labours: it is easy by the commandment to be ordained, that also which was made, might be reserved.
text.KJV: If therefore thou shalt destroy him which with so great labour was fashioned, it is an easy thing to be ordained by thy commandment, that the thing which was made might be preserved.
text.NRSV: If then you will suddenly and quickly] destroy what with so great labour was fashioned by your command, to what purpose was it made?

text.DRB: And now Lord I will speak, of every man thou rather knowest: but concerning thy people, for which I am sorrowful:
text.KJV: Now therefore, Lord, I will speak; touching man in general, thou knowest best; but touching thy people, for whose sake I am sorry;
text.NRSV: And now I will speak out: About all humankind you know best; but I will speak about your people, for whom I am grieved,

text.DRB: and concerning thine inheritance, for which I mourn, and for Israel for whom I am pensive, and concerning Jacob, for whom I am sorrowful.
text.KJV: And for thine inheritance, for whose cause I mourn; and for Israel, for whom I am heavy; and for Jacob, for whose sake I am troubled;
text.NRSV: and about your inheritance, for whom I lament, and about Israel, for whom I am sad, and about the seed of Jacob, for whom I am troubled.

text.DRB: Therefore will I begin to pray before thee for me, and for them: because I see our defaults that inhabit the earth.
text.KJV: Therefore will I begin to pray before thee for myself and for them: for I see the falls of us that dwell in the land.
text.NRSV: Therefore I will pray before you for myself and for them, for I see the failings of us who inhabit the earth;

text.DRB: But I have heard of the celerity of the judge that shall be.
text.KJV: But I have heard the swiftness of the judge which is to come.
text.NRSV: and now also] I have heard of the swiftness of the judgement that is to come.

text.DRB: Therefore hear my voice, and understand my word, and I will speak before thee.
text.KJV: Therefore hear my voice, and understand my words, and I shall speak before thee. This is the beginning of the words of Esdras, before he was taken up: and I said,
text.NRSV: The beginning of the words of Ezra’s prayer,] before he was taken up. He said:

text.DRB: The beginning of the words of Esdras before he was assumpted: and I said: Lord which inhabitest the world, whose eyes are elevated unto things on high and in the air:
text.KJV: O Lord, thou that dwellest in everlastingness which beholdest from above things in the heaven and in the air;
text.NRSV: ‘O Lord, you who inhabit eternity,] whose eyes are exalted] and whose upper chambers are in the air,

text.DRB: and whose throne is inestimable, and glory incomprehensible: by whom standeth an host of Angels with trembling,
text.KJV: Whose throne is inestimable; whose glory may not be comprehended; before whom the hosts of angels stand with trembling,
text.NRSV: whose throne is beyond measure and whose glory is beyond comprehension, before whom the hosts of angels stand trembling

text.DRB: whose keeping is turned in wind and fire, thou whose word is true, and sayings permanent:
text.KJV: Whose service is conversant in wind and fire; whose word is true, and sayings constant; whose commandment is strong, and ordinance fearful;
text.NRSV: and at whose command they are changed to wind and fire,] whose word is sure and whose utterances are certain, whose command is strong and whose ordinance is terrible,

text.DRB: whose commandment is strong, and disposition terrible: whose look dryeth up the depths, and indignation maketh the mountains to melt, and truth doth testify.
text.KJV: Whose look drieth up the depths, and indignation maketh the mountains to melt away; which the truth witnesseth:
text.NRSV: whose look dries up the depths and whose indignation makes the mountains melt away, and whose truth is established] for ever—

text.DRB: Hear the prayer of thy servant, and with thine ears receive the petition of thy creature.
text.KJV: O hear the prayer of thy servant, and give ear to the petition of thy creature.
text.NRSV: hear, O Lord, the prayer of your servant, and give ear to the petition of your creature; attend to my words.

text.DRB: For whilst I live, I will speak: & whilst I understand, I will answer:
text.KJV: For while I live I will speak, and so long as I have understanding I will answer.
text.NRSV: For as long as I live I will speak, and as long as I have understanding I will answer.

text.DRB: Neither do thou respect the sins of thy people, but them that serve thee in truth.
text.KJV: O look not upon the sins of thy people; but on them which serve thee in truth.
text.NRSV: O do not look on the sins of your people, but on those who serve you in truth.

text.DRB: Neither do thou attend the impious endeavours of the nations, but them that with sorrows have kept thy testimonies.
text.KJV: Regard not the wicked inventions of the heathen, but the desire of those that keep thy testimonies in afflictions.
text.NRSV: Do not take note of the endeavours of those who act wickedly, but of the endeavours of those who have kept your covenants amid afflictions.

text.DRB: Neither think thou of them that in thy sight have converst falsely, but remember them that according to thy will have known thy fear.
text.KJV: Think not upon those that have walked feignedly before thee: but remember them, which according to thy will have known thy fear.
text.NRSV: Do not think of those who have lived wickedly in your sight, but remember those who have willingly acknowledged that you are to be feared.

text.DRB: Neither be thou willing to destroy them that have had the manners of beasts: but respect them that have taught thy law gloriously.
text.KJV: Let it not be thy will to destroy them which have lived like beasts; but to look upon them that have clearly taught thy law.
text.NRSV: Do not will the destruction of those who have the ways of cattle, but regard those who have gloriously taught your law.]

text.DRB: Neither have indignation towards them, which are judged worse than beasts: but love them that always have confidence in thy justice and glory.
text.KJV: Take thou no indignation at them which are deemed worse than beasts; but love them that always put their trust in thy righteousness and glory.
text.NRSV: Do not be angry with those who are deemed worse than wild animals, but love those who have always put their trust in your glory.

text.DRB: Because we and our fathers languish with such diseases: but thou for sinners shalt be called merciful.
text.KJV: For we and our fathers do languish of such diseases: but because of us sinners thou shalt be called merciful.
text.NRSV: For we and our ancestors have passed our lives in ways that bring death;] but it is because of us sinners that you are called merciful.

text.DRB: For if thou shalt be desirous to have mercy on us, then thou shalt be called merciful, to us having no works of justice.
text.KJV: For if thou hast a desire to have mercy upon us, thou shalt be called merciful, to us namely, that have no works of righteousness.
text.NRSV: For if you have desired to have pity on us, who have no works of righteousness, then you will be called merciful.

text.DRB: For the just which
text.KJV: For the just, which have many good works laid up with thee, shall out of their own deeds receive reward.
text.NRSV: For the righteous, who have many works laid up with you, shall receive their reward in consequence of their own deeds.

text.DRB: For what is man, that thou art angry with him: or the corruptible kind, that thou art so bitter touching it?
text.KJV: For what is man, that thou shouldest take displeasure at him? or what is a corruptible generation, that thou shouldest be so bitter toward it?
text.NRSV: But what are mortals, that you are angry with them; or what is a corruptible race, that you are so bitter against it?
