text.DRB: this is the interpretation: these are they whom the Highest hath kept to their end, this is a small Kingdom, and full of trouble.
text.KJV: It signifieth that these are they, whom the Highest hath kept unto their end: this is the small kingdom and full of trouble, as thou sawest.
text.NRSV: this is the interpretation: It is these whom the Most High has kept for the eagle’s] end; this was the reign which was brief and full of tumult, as you have seen.

text.KJV: And the lion, whom thou sawest rising up out of the wood, and roaring, and speaking to the eagle, and rebuking her for her unrighteousness with all the words which thou hast heard;
text.NRSV: “As for the lion whom you saw rousing up out of the forest and roaring and speaking to the eagle... he is the Messiah whom the Most High has kept until the end of days.”
text.DRB: As thou sawest the lion also, whom thou sawest awaking out of the wood, and roaring, and speaking to the eagle, and rebuking her, and her injustices by all his words as thou hast heard:
this is the wind which the Highest hath kept unto the end for them, and their impieties: and he shall rebuke them, and shall cast in their spoils before them.
text.KJV: This is the anointed, which the Highest hath kept for them and for their wickedness unto the end: he shall reprove them, and shall upbraid them with their cruelty.
text.NRSV: this is the Messiah] whom the Most High has kept until the end of days, who will arise from the offspring of David, and will come and speak] with them. He will denounce them for their ungodliness and for their wickedness, and will display before them their contemptuous dealings.

text.DRB: For he shall set them in judgement alive: and it shall be, when he hath reproved them, then shall he chastise them.
text.KJV: For he shall set them before him alive in judgment, and shall rebuke them, and correct them.
text.NRSV: For first he will bring them alive before his judgement seat, and when he has reproved them, then he will destroy them.

text.DRB: For the rest of my people he shall deliver with misery, them that are saved upon my borders, and he shall make them joyful till the end shall come, the day of judgement, whereof I have spoken to thee from the beginning.
text.KJV: For the rest of my people shall he deliver with mercy, those that have been pressed upon my borders, and he shall make them joyful until the coming of the day of judgment, whereof I have spoken unto thee from the the beginning.
text.NRSV: But in mercy he will set free the remnant of my people, those who have been saved throughout my borders, and he will make them joyful until the end comes, the day of judgement, of which I spoke to you at the beginning.

text.DRB: This is the dream which thou sawest, and these be the interpretations.
text.KJV: This is the dream that thou sawest, and these are the interpretations.
text.NRSV: This is the dream that you saw, and this is its interpretation.

text.DRB: Thou therefore only hast been worthy to know this secret of the Highest.
text.KJV: Thou only hast been meet to know this secret of the Highest.
text.NRSV: And you alone were worthy to learn this secret of the Most High.

text.DRB: Write therefore in a Book all these things which thou hast seen, and put them in a hidden place:
text.KJV: Therefore write all these things that thou hast seen in a book, and hide them:
text.NRSV: Therefore write all these things that you have seen in a book, put it] in a hidden place;

text.DRB: and thou shalt teach them the wise men of thy people, whose hearts thou knowest able to take, & to keep these secrets.
text.KJV: And teach them to the wise of the people, whose hearts thou knowest may comprehend and keep these secrets.
text.NRSV: and you shall teach them to the wise among your people, whose hearts you know are able to comprehend and keep these secrets.

text.DRB: But do thou stay here yet other seven days, that there may be shewed thee whatsoever shall seem good to the Highest to shew thee.
text.KJV: But wait thou here thyself yet seven days more, that it may be shewed thee, whatsoever it pleaseth the Highest to declare unto thee. And with that he went his way.
text.NRSV: But as for you, wait here for seven days more, so that you may be shown whatever it pleases the Most High to show you.’ Then he left me.

text.DRB: And he departed from me, And it came to pass, when all the people had heard that the seven days were past, and I had not returned into the city, and all gathered themselves together from the least unto the greatest: and came to me, and spake to me saying:
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when all the people saw that the seven days were past, and I not come again into the city, they gathered them all together, from the least unto the greatest, and came unto me, and said,
text.NRSV: When all the people heard that the seven days were past and I had not returned to the city, they all gathered together, from the least to the greatest, and came to me and spoke to me, saying,

text.DRB: What have we sinned to thee, or what have we done unjustly against thee, that leaving us thou hast sitten in this place?
text.KJV: What have we offended thee? and what evil have we done against thee, that thou forsakest us, and sittest here in this place?
text.NRSV: ‘How have we offended you, and what harm have we done you, that you have forsaken us and sit in this place?

text.DRB: For thou alone art remaining to us of all peoples, as a cluster of grapes of the vineyard, and as a candle in a dark place, and as an haven and ship saved from the tempest.
text.KJV: For of all the prophets thou only art left us, as a cluster of the vintage, and as a candle in a dark place, and as a haven or ship preserved from the tempest.
text.NRSV: For of all the prophets you alone are left to us, like a cluster of grapes from the vintage, and like a lamp in a dark place, and like a haven for a ship saved from a storm.

text.DRB: Or are not the evils that chance, sufficient for us?
text.KJV: Are not the evils which are come to us sufficient?
text.NRSV: Are not the disasters that have befallen us enough?

text.DRB: If then thou shalt forsake us, how much better had it been to us, if we also had been burnt with the burning of Sion?
text.KJV: If thou shalt forsake us, how much better had it been for us, if we also had been burned in the midst of Sion?
text.NRSV: Therefore if you forsake us, how much better it would have been for us if we also had been consumed in the burning of Zion.

text.DRB: For we are not better than they that died there. And they wept with a loud voice. And I answered them, & said:
text.KJV: For we are not better than they that died there. And they wept with a loud voice. Then answered I them, and said,
text.NRSV: Then I answered them and said,

text.DRB: Be of good cheer Israel, & be not sorrowful thou house of Jacob.
text.KJV: Be of good comfort, O Israel; and be not heavy, thou house of Jacob:
text.NRSV: ‘Take courage, O Israel; and do not be sorrowful, O house of Jacob;

text.DRB: For there is remembrance of you before the Highest, and the Strong hath not forgotten you in tentation.
text.KJV: For the Highest hath you in remembrance, and the Mighty hath not forgotten you in temptation.
text.NRSV: for the Most High has you in remembrance, and the Mighty One has not forgotten you in your struggle.

text.DRB: For I have not forsaken you, neither did I depart from you: but I came into this place, to pray for the desolation of Sion, and to seek mercy for the low estate of your sanctification.
text.KJV: As for me, I have not forsaken you, neither am I departed from you: but am come into this place, to pray for the desolation of Sion, and that I might seek mercy for the low estate of your sanctuary.
text.NRSV: As for me, I have neither forsaken you nor withdrawn from you; but I have come to this place to pray on account of the desolation of Zion, and to seek mercy on account of the humiliation of our] sanctuary.

text.DRB: And now go every one of you into his house, and I will come to you after these days.
text.KJV: And now go your way home every man, and after these days will I come unto you.
text.NRSV: Now go to your homes, every one of you, and after these days I will come to you.’

text.DRB: And the people departed, as I said to them, into the city:
text.KJV: So the people went their way into the city, like as I commanded them:
text.NRSV: So the people went into the city, as I told them to do.

text.DRB: but I sat in the field seven days, as he commanded me: and I did eat of the flowers of the field only, of the herbs was my meat made in those
text.KJV: But I remained still in the field seven days, as the angel commanded me; and did eat only in those days of the flowers of the field, and had my meat of the herbs
text.NRSV: But I sat in the field for seven days, as the angel] had commanded me; and I ate only of the flowers of the field, and my food was of plants during those days.

text.KJV: And it came to pass after seven days, I dreamed a dream by night:
text.NRSV: After seven days I dreamed a dream in the night.

text.KJV: And, lo, there arose a wind from the sea, that it moved all the waves thereof.
text.NRSV: And behold, a wind arose from the sea and stirred up all its waves.
text.DRB: And behold there rose a wind from the sea, that troubled all the waves thereof.

text.KJV: And I beheld, and, lo, that man waxed strong with the thousands of heaven: and when he turned his countenance to look, all the things trembled that were seen under him.
text.NRSV: I looked, and behold, this wind brought up out of the midst of the sea something like the figure of a man; and I looked, and behold, that man flew with the clouds of heaven.
text.DRB: And I saw, and behold that man grew strong with thousands of heaven: and when he turned his countenance to consider, all things trembled that were seen under him:
& wheresoever voice proceeded out of his mouth, all that heard his voices began to burn, as the earth is quiet when it feeleth the fire.
text.KJV: And whensoever the voice went out of his mouth, all they burned that heard his voice, like as the earth faileth when it feeleth the fire.
text.NRSV: and whenever his voice issued from his mouth, all who heard his voice melted as wax melts] when it feels the fire.

text.DRB: And I saw after these, and behold a multitude of men was gathered together, of whom there was no number, from the four winds of heaven, to fight against the man that was come up out of the sea.
text.KJV: And after this I beheld, and, lo, there was gathered together a multitude of men, out of number, from the four winds of the heaven, to subdue the man that came out of the sea
text.NRSV: After this I looked and saw that an innumerable multitude of people were gathered together from the four winds of heaven to make war against the man who came up out of the sea.

text.DRB: And I saw, and behold he had graven to himself a great mountain, and he flew upon it.
text.KJV: But I beheld, and, lo, he had graved himself a great mountain, and flew up upon it.
text.NRSV: And I looked and saw that he carved out for himself a great mountain, and flew up on to it.

text.DRB: And I sought to see the country, or the place whence the mountain was graven, and I could not.
text.KJV: But I would have seen the region or place whereout the hill was graven, and I could not.
text.NRSV: And I tried to see the region or place from which the mountain was carved, but I could not.

text.DRB: And after these things I saw, and behold all that were gathered to him, to overthrow him, feared exceedingly, yet they were bold to fight.
text.KJV: And after this I beheld, and, lo, all they which were gathered together to subdue him were sore afraid, and yet durst fight.
text.NRSV: After this I looked and saw that all who had gathered together against him, to wage war with him, were filled with fear, and yet they dared to fight.

text.DRB: And behold as he saw the violence of the multitude that came, he lifted not up his hand, nor held sword, nor any warlike instrument, but only as I saw,
text.KJV: And, lo, as he saw the violence of the multitude that came, he neither lifted up his hand, nor held sword, nor any instrument of war:
text.NRSV: When he saw the onrush of the approaching multitude, he neither lifted his hand nor held a spear or any weapon of war;

text.DRB: that he sent forth out of his mouth as it were a blast of fire, and from his lips a spirit of flame, & from his tongue he sent forth sparkles and tempests, & all things were mingled together with this blast of fire, and spirit of flame, and multitude of tempests.
text.KJV: But only I saw that he sent out of his mouth as it had been a blast of fire, and out of his lips a flaming breath, and out of his tongue he cast out sparks and tempests.
text.NRSV: but I saw only how he sent forth from his mouth something like a stream of fire, and from his lips a flaming breath, and from his tongue he shot forth a storm of sparks.]

text.DRB: And it fell with violence upon the multitude, that was prepared to fight, and burned them all, that suddenly there was nothing seen of an innumerable multitude, but only dust, and the savour of smoke: and I saw, and was afraid.
text.KJV: And they were all mixed together; the blast of fire, the flaming breath, and the great tempest; and fell with violence upon the multitude which was prepared to fight, and burned them up every one, so that upon a sudden of an innumerable multitude nothing was to be perceived, but only dust and smell of smoke: when I saw this I was afraid.
text.NRSV: All these were mingled together, the stream of fire and the flaming breath and the great storm, and fell on the onrushing multitude that was prepared to fight, and burned up all of them, so that suddenly nothing was seen of the innumerable multitude but only the dust of ashes and the smell of smoke. When I saw it, I was amazed.

text.DRB: And after these things I saw the man himself descending from the mountain, and calling to him another peaceable multitude,
text.KJV: Afterward saw I the same man come down from the mountain, and call unto him another peaceable Multitude.
text.NRSV: After this I saw the same man come down from the mountain and call to himself another multitude that was peaceable.

text.DRB: and there came to him the countenance of many men, some rejoicing, and some sorrowing: and some bond, some bringing of them that were offered. And I was sick for much fear, & awaked, and said.
text.KJV: And there came much people unto him, whereof some were glad, some were sorry, and some of them were bound, and other some brought of them that were offered: then was I sick through great fear, and I awaked, and said,
text.NRSV: Then I woke up in great terror, and prayed to the Most High, and said,

text.DRB: Thou from the beginning hast shewed thy servant these marvelous things, and hast counted me worthy that thou wouldest receive my petition.
text.KJV: Thou hast shewed thy servant these wonders from the beginning, and hast counted me worthy that thou shouldest receive my prayer:
text.NRSV: ‘From the beginning you have shown your servant these wonders, and have deemed me worthy to have my prayer heard by you;

text.DRB: And now shew me yet the interpretation of this dream.
text.KJV: Shew me now yet the interpretation of this dream.
text.NRSV: now show me the interpretation of this dream also.

text.DRB: For as I think in my judgement, woe to them that were left in those days: and much more woe to them that were not left.
text.KJV: For as I conceive in mine understanding, woe unto them that shall be left in those days and much more woe unto them that are not left behind!
text.NRSV: For as I consider it in my mind, alas for those who will be left in those days! And still more, alas for those who are not left!

text.DRB: For they that were not left, were sorrowful.
text.KJV: For they that were not left were in heaviness.
text.NRSV: For those who are not left will be sad

text.DRB: I understand now what things are laid up in the later days, and they shall happen to them, yea and to them that are left.
text.KJV: Now understand I the things that are laid up in the latter days, which shall happen unto them, and to those that are left behind.
text.NRSV: because they understand the things that are reserved for the last days, but cannot attain them.

text.DRB: For therefore they came into great dangers, and many necessities, as these dreams do shew.
text.KJV: Therefore are they come into great perils and many necessities, like as these dreams declare.
text.NRSV: But alas for those also who are left, and for that very reason! For they shall see great dangers and much distress, as these dreams show.

text.DRB: But yet it is easier, adventuring to come into it, than to pass, as a cloud from the world, and not to see the things that happen in the later time. And he answered me, and said:
text.KJV: Yet is it easier for him that is in danger to come into these things, than to pass away as a cloud out of the world, and not to see the things that happen in the last days. And he answered unto me, and said,
text.NRSV: He answered me and said,

text.DRB: Both the interpretation of the vision I will tell thee: and also concerning the things that thou hast spoken I will open to thee.
text.KJV: The interpretation of the vision shall I shew thee, and I will open unto thee the thing that thou hast required.
text.NRSV: ‘I will tell you the interpretation of the vision, and I will also explain to you the things that you have mentioned.

text.DRB: Whereas thou speakest of them that were left, this is the interpretation.
text.KJV: Whereas thou hast spoken of them that are left behind, this is the interpretation:
text.NRSV: As for what you said about those who survive, and concerning those who do not survive,] this is the interpretation:

text.DRB: He that taketh away danger at that time, he hath guarded himself. They that have fallen into danger, these are they that have works, and faith in the Strongest.
text.KJV: He that shall endure the peril in that time hath kept himself: they that be fallen into danger are such as have works, and faith toward the Almighty.
text.NRSV: The one who brings the peril at that time will protect those who fall into peril, who have works and faith towards the Almighty.

text.DRB: Know therefore that they are more blessed which are left, than they that are dead.
text.KJV: Know this therefore, that they which be left behind are more blessed than they that be dead.
text.NRSV: Understand therefore that those who are left are more blessed than those who have died.

text.KJV: This is the meaning of the vision: Whereas thou sawest a man coming up from the midst of the sea:
text.NRSV: “This is the interpretation of the vision: As for your seeing a man coming up from the heart of the sea,”
text.DRB: These are the interpretations of the vision, whereas thou sawest a man coming from the heart of the sea,

text.KJV: The same is he whom God the Highest hath kept a great season, which by his own self shall deliver his creature: and he shall order them that are left behind.
text.NRSV: “this is he whom the Most High has been keeping for many ages, who will himself deliver his creation; and he will direct those who are left.”
text.DRB: the same is he whom the Highest preserveth much time, which by himself shall deliver his creature: and he shall dispose them that are left.
And whereas thou sawest proceed out of his mouth, as it were wind, and fire, and tempest:
text.KJV: And whereas thou sawest, that out of his mouth there came as a blast of wind, and fire, and storm;
text.NRSV: And as for your seeing wind and fire and a storm coming out of his mouth,

text.DRB: and whereas he held no sword, nor warlike instrument: for his violence destroyed the multitude that came to overthrow him: this is the interpretation.
text.KJV: And that he held neither sword, nor any instrument of war, but that the rushing in of him destroyed the whole multitude that came to subdue him; this is the interpretation:
text.NRSV: and as for his not holding a spear or weapon of war, yet destroying the onrushing multitude that came to conquer him, this is the interpretation:

text.DRB: Behold the days come, when the Highest shall begin to deliver them, that are upon the earth:
text.KJV: Behold, the days come, when the most High will begin to deliver them that are upon the earth.
text.NRSV: The days are coming when the Most High will deliver those who are on the earth.

text.DRB: and he shall come in excess of mind upon them that inhabit the earth.
text.KJV: And he shall come to the astonishment of them that dwell on the earth.
text.NRSV: And bewilderment of mind shall come over those who inhabit the earth.

text.DRB: And one shall think
text.KJV: And one shall undertake to fight against another, one city against another, one place against another, one people against another, and one realm against another.
text.NRSV: They shall plan to make war against one another, city against city, place against place, people against people, and kingdom against kingdom.

text.DRB: And it shall be, when these things shall come to pass, and the signs shall happen, which I shewed thee before: and then shall my son be revealed, whom thou sawest, as a man coming up.
text.KJV: And the time shall be when these things shall come to pass, and the signs shall happen which I shewed thee before, and then shall my Son be declared, whom thou sawest as a man ascending.
text.NRSV: When these things take place and the signs occur that I showed you before, then my Son will be revealed, whom you saw as a man coming up from the sea.]

text.DRB: And it shall be when all nations shall hear his voice: and every one in his country shall leave their war, that they have toward each other:
text.KJV: And when all the people hear his voice, every man shall in their own land leave the battle they have one against another.
text.NRSV: ‘Then, when all the nations hear his voice, all the nations shall leave their own lands and the warfare that they have against one another;

text.DRB: and an innumerable multitude shall be gathered in one, as willing to come to overthrow him.
text.KJV: And an innumerable multitude shall be gathered together, as thou sawest them, willing to come, and to overcome him by fighting.
text.NRSV: and an innumerable multitude shall be gathered together, as you saw, wishing to come and conquer him.

text.DRB: But he shall stand upon the top of mount Sion.
text.KJV: But he shall stand upon the top of the mount Sion.
text.NRSV: But he shall stand on the top of Mount Zion.

text.DRB: And Sion shall come, and it shall be shewed to all prepared and builded, as thou sawest the mountain to be graven without hands.
text.KJV: And Sion shall come, and shall be shewed to all men, being prepared and builded, like as thou sawest the hill graven without hands.
text.NRSV: And Zion shall come and be made manifest to all people, prepared and built, as you saw the mountain carved out without hands.

text.DRB: And the same my son shall reprove the things that the Gentiles have invented, these their impieties which came near to the tempest, because of their evil cogitations, and torments wherewith they shall begin to be tormented.
text.KJV: And this my Son shall rebuke the wicked inventions of those nations, which for their wicked life are fallen into the tempest;
text.NRSV: Then he, my Son, will reprove the assembled nations for their ungodliness (this was symbolized by the storm),

text.DRB: Which were likened to the flame, and he shall destroy them without labour by the law that was likened to the fire.
text.KJV: And shall lay before them their evil thoughts, and the torments wherewith they shall begin to be tormented, which are like unto a flame: and he shall destroy them without labour by the law which is like unto me.
text.NRSV: and will reproach them to their face with their evil thoughts and the torments with which they are to be tortured (which were symbolized by the flames), and will destroy them without effort by means of the law] (which was symbolized by the fire).

text.DRB: And whereas thou sawest him gathering unto him another peaceable multitude.
text.KJV: And whereas thou sawest that he gathered another peaceable multitude unto him;
text.NRSV: ‘And as for your seeing him gather to himself another multitude that was peaceable,

text.DRB: These are the ten tribes, which were made captive out of their land in the days of Osee the king, whom Salmanasar the king of the Assyrians led captive: and he transported them beyond the river, and they were transported into another land. 4. .
text.KJV: Those are the ten tribes, which were carried away prisoners out of their own land in the time of Osea the king, whom Salmanasar the king of Assyria led away captive, and he carried them over the waters, and so came they into another land.
text.NRSV: these are the nine] tribes that were taken away from their own land into exile in the days of King Hoshea, whom Shalmaneser, king of the Assyrians, made captives; he took them across the river, and they were taken into another land.

text.DRB: But they gave themselves this counsel, to forsake the multitude of nations, and to go forth into a farther country, where mankind never inhabited.
text.KJV: But they took this counsel among themselves, that they would leave the multitude of the heathen, and go forth into a further country, where never mankind dwelt,
text.NRSV: But they formed this plan for themselves, that they would leave the multitude of the nations and go to a more distant region, where no human beings had ever lived,

text.DRB: Or there to observe their ordinances, which they had not kept in their country.
text.KJV: That they might there keep their statutes, which they never kept in their own land.
text.NRSV: so that there at least they might keep their statutes that they had not kept in their own land.

text.DRB: And they entered in by the narrow entrances of the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: And they entered into Euphrates by the narrow places of the river.
text.NRSV: And they went in by the narrow passages of the Euphrates river.

text.DRB: For the Highest then wrought them signs, and stayed the veins of the river till they passed. Exod. 14. Iosue. 3.
text.KJV: For the most High then shewed signs for them, and held still the flood, till they were passed over.
text.NRSV: For at that time the Most High performed signs for them, and stopped the channels of the river until they had crossed over.

text.DRB: For by that country was a great way to go, of one year and a half: for the country is called Arsareth.
text.KJV: For through that country there was a great way to go, namely, of a year and a half: and the same region is called Arsareth.
text.NRSV: Through that region there was a long way to go, a journey of a year and a half; and that country is called Arzareth.]

text.DRB: Then did they inhabit there till in the later time: and now again when they begin to come,
text.KJV: Then dwelt they there until the latter time; and now when they shall begin to come,
text.NRSV: ‘Then they lived there until the last times; and now, when they are about to come again,

text.DRB: again the Highest shall stay the veins of the river, that they may pass: for these thou sawest a multitude with peace.
text.KJV: The Highest shall stay the springs of the stream again, that they may go through: therefore sawest thou the multitude with peace.
text.NRSV: the Most High will stop] the channels of the river again, so that they may be able to cross over. Therefore you saw the multitude gathered together in peace.

text.DRB: But they also that were left of the people, these are they that be within my border.
text.KJV: But those that be left behind of thy people are they that are found within my borders.
text.NRSV: But those who are left of your people, who are found within my holy borders, shall be saved.]

text.DRB: It shall come to pass therefore, when he shall begin to destroy the multitude of these nations, that are gathered, he shall protect them that have overcome the people:
text.KJV: Now when he destroyeth the multitude of the nations that are gathered together, he shall defend his people that remain.
text.NRSV: Therefore when he destroys the multitude of the nations that are gathered together, he will defend the people who remain.

text.DRB: and then shall he shew them very many wonders.
text.KJV: And then shall he shew them great wonders.
text.NRSV: And then he will show them very many wonders.’

text.DRB: And I said: Lord dominatour, shew me this, why I saw a man coming up from the heart of the sea, and he said to me:
text.KJV: Then said I, O Lord that bearest rule, shew me this: Wherefore have I seen the man coming up from the midst of the sea?
text.NRSV: I said, ‘O sovereign Lord, explain this to me: Why did I see the man coming up from the heart of the sea?’

text.DRB: As thou canst not either search these things, or know what things are in the depth of the sea: so cannot any man upon the earth see my son, or them that are with him, but in the time of a day.
text.KJV: And he said unto me, Like as thou canst neither seek out nor know the things that are in the deep of the sea: even so can no man upon earth see my Son, or those that be with him, but in the day time.
text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘Just as no one can explore or know what is in the depths of the sea, so no one on earth can see my Son or those who are with him, except in the time of his day.]

text.DRB: This is the interpretation of the dream which thou sawest, and for the which thou only art here illuminated.
text.KJV: This is the interpretation of the dream which thou sawest, and whereby thou only art here lightened.
text.NRSV: This is the interpretation of the dream that you saw. And you alone have been enlightened about this,

text.DRB: For thou hast left thine own law, and hast been occupied about my law, and hast sought it.
text.KJV: For thou hast forsaken thine own way, and applied thy diligence unto my law, and sought it.
text.NRSV: because you have forsaken your own ways and have applied yourself to mine, and have searched out my law;

text.DRB: For thou hast disposed thy life in wisdom, and thine understanding thou hast called mother:
text.KJV: Thy life hast thou ordered in wisdom, and hast called understanding thy mother.
text.NRSV: for you have devoted your life to wisdom, and called understanding your mother.

text.DRB: and for this I have shewed thee riches with the Highest. For it shall be after other three days, I will speak other things to thee, and I will expound to thee weighty and marvelous things.
text.KJV: And therefore have I shewed thee the treasures of the Highest: after other three days I will speak other things unto thee, and declare unto thee mighty and wondrous things.
text.NRSV: Therefore I have shown you these things; for there is a reward laid up with the Most High. For it will be that after three more days I will tell you other things, and explain weighty and wondrous matters to you.’

text.DRB: And I went forth, and passed into the field, much glorifying and praising the Highest for the marvelous things that he did by time.
text.KJV: Then went I forth into the field, giving praise and thanks greatly unto the most High because of his wonders which he did in time;
text.NRSV: Then I got up and walked in the field, giving great glory and praise to the Most High for the wonders that he does] from time to time,

text.DRB: And because he governeth it, and the things that are brought in times, and I sat there three days.
text.KJV: And because he governeth the same, and such things as fall in their seasons: and there I sat three days.
text.NRSV: and because he governs the times and whatever things come to pass in their seasons. And I stayed there for three days.

text.KJV: And it came to pass upon the third day, I sat under an oak, and, behold, there came a voice out of a bush over against me, and said, Esdras, Esdras.
text.NRSV: On the third day, while I was sitting under an oak, behold, a voice came out of a bush opposite me and said, “Ezra, Ezra.”

text.DRB: And behold a voice came forth against me out of a bush, and said: Esdras, Esdras: and I said: Lo here I am Lord. And I arose upon my feet. And he said to me:
text.KJV: And I said, Here am I, Lord And I stood up upon my feet.
text.NRSV: And I answered, ‘Here I am, Lord’, and I rose to my feet.

text.KJV: Then said he unto me, In the bush I did manifestly reveal myself unto Moses, and talked with him, when my people served in Egypt:
text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “I revealed myself in a bush and spoke to Moses when my people were in bondage in Egypt;”
text.DRB: Revealing I was revealed upon the bush, and spake to Moyses, when the people served in Ægypt, Exod. 3.
and I sent him, and brought my people out of Ægypt, and brought him upon mount Sina, and held him with me many days.
text.KJV: And I sent him and led my people out of Egypt, and brought him up to the mount of where I held him by me a long season,
text.NRSV: and I sent him and led] my people out of Egypt; and I led him up on Mount Sinai, where I kept him with me for many days.

text.DRB: And I told him many marvelous things, and shewed him the secrets of times, and the end: and I commanded him, saying:
text.KJV: And told him many wondrous things, and shewed him the secrets of the times, and the end; and commanded him, saying,
text.NRSV: I told him many wondrous things, and showed him the secrets of the times and declared to him] the end of the times. Then I commanded him, saying,

text.DRB: These words thou shalt publish abroad, and these thou shalt hide.
text.KJV: These words shalt thou declare, and these shalt thou hide.
text.NRSV: “These words you shall publish openly, and these you shall keep secret.”

text.DRB: And now to thee I say:
text.KJV: And now I say unto thee,
text.NRSV: And now I say to you:

text.DRB: The signs which I have shewed, and the dreams which thou hast seen, and the interpretations which thou hast seen, lay them up in thy heart.
text.KJV: That thou lay up in thy heart the signs that I have shewed, and the dreams that thou hast seen, and the interpretations which thou hast heard:
text.NRSV: Lay up in your heart the signs that I have shown you, the dreams that you have seen, and the interpretations that you have heard;

text.DRB: For thou shalt be received of all, thou shalt be converted the residue with thy counsel, and with the like to thee, till the times be finished.
text.KJV: For thou shalt be taken away from all, and from henceforth thou shalt remain with my Son, and with such as be like thee, until the times be ended.
text.NRSV: for you shall be taken up from among humankind, and henceforth you shall live with my Son and with those who are like you, until the times are ended.

text.DRB: Because the world hath lost his youth, & the times draw near to wax old.
text.KJV: For the world hath lost his youth, and the times begin to wax old.
text.NRSV: The age has lost its youth, and the times begin to grow old.

text.DRB: For the world is divided by twelve parts, and the tenth part, and half of the tenth part are passed:
text.KJV: For the world is divided into twelve parts, and the ten parts of it are gone already, and half of a tenth part:
text.NRSV: For the age is divided into twelve parts, and nine] of its parts have already passed,

text.DRB: and there remaineth hereafter the half of the tenth part.
text.KJV: And there remaineth that which is after the half of the tenth part.
text.NRSV: as well as half of the tenth part; so two of its parts remain, besides half of the tenth part.]

text.DRB: Now therefore dispose thy house, and correct thy people, & comfort the humble of them, & forsake now corruption.
text.KJV: Now therefore set thine house in order, and reprove thy people, comfort such of them as be in trouble, and now renounce corruption,
text.NRSV: Now therefore, set your house in order, and reprove your people; comfort the lowly among them, and instruct those that are wise.] And now renounce the life that is corruptible,

text.DRB: And it came to pass the third day, and I sat under an oak.
text.KJV: Let go from thee mortal thoughts, cast away the burdens of man, put off now the weak nature,
text.NRSV: and put away from you mortal thoughts; cast away from you the burdens of humankind, and divest yourself now of your weak nature;

text.DRB: for the evils which thou hast seen to have chanced now, worse than these will they do again: . 1. Ioan. 2.
text.KJV: And set aside the thoughts that are most heavy unto thee, and haste thee to flee from these times.
text.NRSV: lay to one side the thoughts that are most grievous to you, and hurry to escape from these times.

text.DRB: for look how much the world shall become weak by age, so much shall evils be multiplied upon the inhabitants.
text.KJV: For yet greater evils than those which thou hast seen happen shall be done hereafter.
text.NRSV: For evils worse than those that you have now seen happen shall take place hereafter.

text.DRB: For truth hath removed itself farther off, and lying hath approached, for now the vision which thou sawest hasteneth to come.
text.KJV: For look how much the world shall be weaker through age, so much the more shall evils increase upon them that dwell therein.
text.NRSV: For the weaker the world becomes through old age, the more shall evils be increased upon its inhabitants.

text.DRB: And I answered, and said before thee o Lord:
text.KJV: For the time is fled far away, and leasing is hard at hand: for now hasteth the vision to come, which thou hast seen.
text.NRSV: Truth shall go farther away, and falsehood shall come near. For the eagle] that you saw in the vision is already hurrying to come.’

text.DRB: For behold I will go, as thou hast commanded me, & will rebuke the people that now is. But them that shall yet be born, who shall admonish?
text.KJV: Then answered I before thee, and said,
text.NRSV: Then I answered and said, ‘Let me speak] in your presence, Lord.

text.DRB: The world therefore is set in darkness, and they that dwell in it without light.
text.KJV: Behold, Lord, I will go, as thou hast commanded me, and reprove the people which are present: but they that shall be born afterward, who shall admonish them? thus the world is set in darkness, and they that dwell therein are without light.
text.NRSV: For I will go, as you have commanded me, and I will reprove the people who are now living; but who will warn those who will be born hereafter? For the world lies in darkness, and its inhabitants are without light.

text.DRB: Because thy law is burnt, therefore no man knoweth the works that have been done by thee, or that shall begin.
text.KJV: For thy law is burnt, therefore no man knoweth the things that are done of thee, or the work that shall begin.
text.NRSV: For your law has been burned, and so no one knows the things which have been done or will be done by you.

text.DRB: For if I have found grace with thee, send the Holy Ghost to me, & I will write all that hath been done in the world from the beginning, the things that were written in thy law, that men may find the path: and they that will live in the later times, may live.
text.KJV: But if I have found grace before thee, send the Holy Ghost into me, and I shall write all that hath been done in the world since the beginning, which were written in thy law, that men may find thy path, and that they which will live in the latter days may live.
text.NRSV: If then I have found favour with you, send the holy spirit into me, and I will write everything that has happened in the world from the beginning, the things that were written in your law, so that people may be able to find the path, and that those who want to live in the last days may do so.’

text.DRB: And he answered me, and said: Go gather together the people, and thou shalt say to them, that they seek thee not for forty days.
text.KJV: And he answered me, saying, Go thy way, gather the people together, and say unto them, that they seek thee not for forty days.
text.NRSV: He answered me and said, ‘Go and gather the people, and tell them not to seek you for forty days.

text.KJV: But look thou prepare thee many box trees, and take with thee Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Ecanus, and Asiel, these five which are ready to write swiftly;
text.NRSV: “But prepare for yourself many writing tablets, and take with you Sarea, Dabria, Selemia, Ethanus, and Asiel—these five, who are trained to write rapidly;”
text.DRB: And do thou prepare thee many tables of box, and take with thee Sarea, Dabria, Salemia, Echanus, and Asiel, these five which are ready to write swiftly.
And come hither, and I will light in thy heart a candle of understanding, which shall not be put out till the things be finished, which thou shalt begin to write.
text.KJV: And come hither, and I shall light a candle of understanding in thine heart, which shall not be put out, till the things be performed which thou shalt begin to write.
text.NRSV: and you shall come here, and I will light in your heart the lamp of understanding, which shall not be put out until what you are about to write is finished.

text.DRB: And then some things thou shalt open to the perfect, some thou shalt deliver secretly to the wise. For tomorrow this hour thou shalt begin to write.
text.KJV: And when thou hast done, some things shalt thou publish, and some things shalt thou shew secretly to the wise: to morrow this hour shalt thou begin to write.
text.NRSV: And when you have finished, some things you shall make public, and some you shall deliver in secret to the wise; tomorrow at this hour you shall begin to write.’

text.DRB: And I went as he commanded me, and gathered together all the people, and said:
text.KJV: Then went I forth, as he commanded, and gathered all the people together, and said,
text.NRSV: Then I went as he commanded me, and I gathered all the people together, and said,

text.DRB: Hear Israel these words:
text.KJV: Hear these words, O Israel.
text.NRSV: ‘Hear these words, O Israel.

text.DRB: Our fathers were pilgrims from the beginning in Ægypt, and were delivered from thence. . . Act. 7.
text.KJV: Our fathers at the beginning were strangers in Egypt, from whence they were delivered:
text.NRSV: At first our ancestors lived as aliens in Egypt, and they were liberated from there

text.DRB: And they received the law of life, which they kept not, which you also after them have transgressed:
text.KJV: And received the law of life, which they kept not, which ye also have transgressed after them.
text.NRSV: and received the law of life, which they did not keep, which you also have transgressed after them.

text.DRB: and the land was given you by lot, and the land of Sion, and your fathers, and you have done iniquity, and have not kept the ways which the Highest commanded you.
text.KJV: Then was the land, even the land of Sion, parted among you by lot: but your fathers, and ye yourselves, have done unrighteousness, and have not kept the ways which the Highest commanded you.
text.NRSV: Then land was given to you for a possession in the land of Zion; but you and your ancestors committed iniquity and did not keep the ways that the Most High commanded you.

text.DRB: And whereas he is a just judge, he hath taken from you in time that which he had given.
text.KJV: And forasmuch as he is a righteous judge, he took from you in time the thing that he had given you.
text.NRSV: And since he is a righteous judge, in due time he took from you what he had given.

text.DRB: And now you are here, and your brethren are among you.
text.KJV: And now are ye here, and your brethren among you.
text.NRSV: And now you are here, and your people] are farther in the interior.]

text.DRB: If then you will rule over your sense, & instruct your heart, you shall be preserved alive, and after death shall obtain mercy.
text.KJV: Therefore if so be that ye will subdue your own understanding, and reform your hearts, ye shall be kept alive and after death ye shall obtain mercy.
text.NRSV: If you, then, will rule over your minds and discipline your hearts, you shall be kept alive, and after death you shall obtain mercy.

text.DRB: For the judgement shall come after death, when we shall return to life again: and then the names of the just shall appear, and the deeds of the impious shall be shewed.
text.KJV: For after death shall the judgment come, when we shall live again: and then shall the names of the righteous be manifest, and the works of the ungodly shall be declared.
text.NRSV: For after death the judgement will come, when we shall live again; and then the names of the righteous shall become manifest, and the deeds of the ungodly shall be disclosed.

text.DRB: Let no man therefore come to me now, nor
text.KJV: Let no man therefore come unto me now, nor seek after me these forty days.
text.NRSV: But let no one come to me now, and let no one seek me for forty days.’

text.DRB: And I took the five men, as he commanded me, and we went forth into the field, and tarried there.
text.KJV: So I took the five men, as he commanded me, and we went into the field, and remained there.
text.NRSV: So I took the five men, as he commanded me, and we proceeded to the field, and remained there.

text.DRB: And I was come to the morrow, and behold a voice called me, saying: Esdras open thy mouth, and drink that which I will give thee to drink. Ezech. 3.
text.KJV: And the next day, behold, a voice called me, saying, Esdras, open thy mouth, and drink that I give thee to drink.
text.NRSV: And on the next day a voice called me, saying, ‘Ezra, open your mouth and drink what I give you to drink.’

text.DRB: And I opened my mouth, and behold a full cup was brought me, this was full as it were with water: but the colour thereof like as fire.
text.KJV: Then opened I my mouth, and, behold, he reached me a full cup, which was full as it were with water, but the colour of it was like fire.
text.NRSV: So I opened my mouth, and a full cup was offered to me; it was full of something like water, but its colour was like fire.

text.DRB: And I took it, and drank, and when I had drunken of it, my heart was tormented with understanding, and wisdom grew into my breast. For my spirit was kept by memory.
text.KJV: And I took it, and drank: and when I had drunk of it, my heart uttered understanding, and wisdom grew in my breast, for my spirit strengthened my memory:
text.NRSV: I took it and drank; and when I had drunk it, my heart poured forth understanding, and wisdom increased in my breast, for my spirit retained its memory,

text.DRB: And my mouth was opened, and was shut no more.
text.KJV: And my mouth was opened, and shut no more.
text.NRSV: and my mouth was opened and was no longer closed.

text.DRB: The Highest gave understanding unto the five men, and they wrote excesses of the night which were spoken, which they knew not.
text.KJV: The Highest gave understanding unto the five men, and they wrote the wonderful visions of the night that were told, which they knew not: and they sat forty days, and they wrote in the day, and at night they ate bread.
text.NRSV: Moreover, the Most High gave understanding to the five men, and by turns they wrote what was dictated, using characters that they did not know.] They sat for forty days; they wrote during the daytime, and ate their bread at night.

text.DRB: And at night they did eat bread, but I spake by day, and by night held not my peace.
text.KJV: As for me. I spake in the day, and I held not my tongue by night.
text.NRSV: But as for me, I spoke in the daytime and was not silent at night.

text.KJV: In forty days they wrote two hundred and four books.
text.NRSV: “In forty days ninety-four books were written.”
text.DRB: And there were written in the forty days two hundred four books.

text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the forty days were filled, that the Highest spake, saying, The first that thou hast written publish openly, that the worthy and unworthy may read it:
text.NRSV: “And when the forty days were ended, the Most High spoke to me, saying, ‘Make public the twenty-four books that you wrote first, and let the worthy and the unworthy read them;’”
text.DRB: And it came to pass when they had ended the forty days, the Highest spake, saying:

text.KJV: But keep the seventy last, that thou mayest deliver them only to such as be wise among the people:
text.NRSV: “‘but keep the seventy that were written last, in order to give them to the wise among your people.’”
text.DRB: The former things which thou hast written, set abroad, and let the worthy and unworthy read: but the last seventy books thou shalt keep, that thou mayest deliver them to the wise of thy people.
For in these is the vein of understanding, and the fountain of wisdom, and the stream of knowledge. And I did so.
text.KJV: For in them is the spring of understanding, the fountain of wisdom, and the stream of knowledge.
text.NRSV: For in them is the spring of understanding, the fountain of wisdom, and the river of knowledge.’

text.KJV: Behold, speak thou in the ears of my people the words of prophecy, which I will put in thy mouth, saith the Lord:
text.NRSV: The Lord says, “Behold, speak in the ears of my people the words of the prophecy that I will put in your mouth,”

text.KJV: And cause them to be written in paper: for they are faithful and true.
text.NRSV: “and cause them to be written on paper; for they are trustworthy and true.”
text.DRB: and see that they be written in paper, because they be faithful and true.
Be not afraid of the cogitations against thee, neither let the incredulities trouble thee of them that speak.
text.KJV: Fear not the imaginations against thee, let not the incredulity of them trouble thee, that speak against thee.
text.NRSV: Do not fear the plots against you, and do not be troubled by the unbelief of those who oppose you.

text.DRB: Because every incredulous person shall die in his incredulity.
text.KJV: For all the unfaithful shall die in their unfaithfulness.
text.NRSV: For all unbelievers shall die in their unbelief.]

text.DRB: Behold I bring in, saith our Lord, upon the whole earth evils, sword, and famine, and death, and destruction.
text.KJV: Behold, saith the Lord, I will bring plagues upon the world; the sword, famine, death, and destruction.
text.NRSV: Beware, says the Lord, I am bringing evils upon the world, the sword and famine, death and destruction,

text.DRB: Because iniquity hath fully polluted over all the earth, and their hurtful works are accomplished.
text.KJV: For wickedness hath exceedingly polluted the whole earth, and their hurtful works are fulfilled.
text.NRSV: because iniquity has spread throughout every land, and their harmful doings have reached their limit.

text.DRB: Therefore saith our Lord:
text.KJV: Therefore saith the Lord,
text.NRSV: Therefore, says the Lord,

text.KJV: I will hold my tongue no more as touching their wickedness, which they profanely commit, neither will I suffer them in those things, in which they wickedly exercise themselves: behold, the innocent and righteous blood crieth unto me, and the souls of the just complain continually.
text.NRSV: “I will no longer keep silence regarding their impious deeds which they have ungodly committed, nor will I tolerate their wicked practices.”
text.DRB: I will not now keep silence of their impieties which they do irreligiously, neither will I bear with those things, which they practise unjustly. Behold the innocent and just blood cryeth to me, & the souls of the just cry continually.
Revenging I will revenge them, saith our Lord, and I will take all innocent blood out of them unto me. Apoc. 6. v. 10.
text.KJV: And therefore, saith the Lord, I will surely avenge them, and receive unto me all the innocent blood from among them.
text.NRSV: I will surely avenge them, says the Lord, and will receive to myself all the innocent blood from among them.

text.DRB: Behold my people is led to slaughter as a flock, I will no more suffer it to dwell in the land of Ægypt.
text.KJV: Behold, my people is led as a flock to the slaughter: I will not suffer them now to dwell in the land of Egypt:
text.NRSV: See, my people are being led like a flock to the slaughter; I will not allow them to live any longer in the land of Egypt,

text.DRB: But I will bring them forth in a mighty hand and valiant arm, and will strike with plague as before, & will corrupt all the land thereof.
text.KJV: But I will bring them with a mighty hand and a stretched out arm, and smite Egypt with plagues, as before, and will destroy all the land thereof.
text.NRSV: but I will bring them out with a mighty hand and with an uplifted arm, and will strike Egypt with plagues, as before, and will destroy all its land.

text.DRB: Ægypt shall mourn, & the foundations thereof beaten with plague, and with the chastisement which God will bring upon it.
text.KJV: Egypt shall mourn, and the foundation of it shall be smitten with the plague and punishment that God shall bring upon it.
text.NRSV: Let Egypt mourn, and its foundations, because of the plague of chastisement and castigation that the Lord will bring upon it.

text.DRB: The husbandmen that till the ground shall mourn, because their seeds shall perish by blasting, and hail, and by a terrible star.
text.KJV: They that till the ground shall mourn: for their seeds shall fail through the blasting and hail, and with a fearful constellation.
text.NRSV: Let the farmers that till the ground mourn, because their seed shall fail to grow] and their trees shall be ruined by blight and hail and by a terrible tempest.

text.DRB: Woe to the world and them that dwell therein. . Luc. 21.
text.KJV: Woe to the world and them that dwell therein!
text.NRSV: Alas for the world and for those who live in it!

text.DRB: Because the sword is at hand and the destruction of them, and nation shall rise up against nation to fight, and sword in their hands.
text.KJV: For the sword and their destruction draweth nigh, and one people shall stand up and fight against another, and swords in their hands.
text.NRSV: For the sword and misery draw near them, and nation shall rise up to fight against nation, with swords in their hands.

text.DRB: For there shall be instability to men, and growing one against another they shall not care for their king, & the Princes of the way of their doings, in their might.
text.KJV: For there shall be sedition among men, and invading one another; they shall not regard their kings nor princes, and the course of their actions shall stand in their power.
text.NRSV: For there shall be unrest among people; growing strong against one another, they shall in their might have no respect for their king or the chief of their leaders.

text.DRB: For a man shall desire to go into the city and cannot.
text.KJV: A man shall desire to go into a city, and shall not be able.
text.NRSV: For a person will desire to go into a city, and shall not be able to do so.

text.DRB: Because of their prides the cities shall be troubled, the houses raised, the men shall fear.
text.KJV: For because of their pride the cities shall be troubled, the houses shall be destroyed, and men shall be afraid.
text.NRSV: Because of their pride the cities shall be in confusion, the houses shall be destroyed, and people shall be afraid.

text.DRB: Man shall not pity his neighbour, to make their houses nothing worth in the sword, to spoil their goods for famine of bread, & much tribulation.
text.KJV: A man shall have no pity upon his neighbour, but shall destroy their houses with the sword, and spoil their goods, because of the lack of bread, and for great tribulation.
text.NRSV: People shall have no pity for their neighbours, but shall make an assault upon] their houses with the sword, and plunder their goods, because of hunger for bread and because of great tribulation.

text.DRB: Behold, I call together, saith God, all the kings of the earth to fear me, that are from the Orient, and from the South, from the East, and from
text.KJV: Behold, saith God, I will call together all the kings of the earth to reverence me, which are from the rising of the sun, from the south, from the east, and Libanus; to turn themselves one against another, and repay the things that they have done to them.
text.NRSV: See how I am calling together all the kings of the earth to turn to me, says God, from the rising sun and from the south, from the east and from Lebanon; to turn and repay what they have given them.

text.DRB: As they do until this day to mine elect, so will I do, and render in their bosom. Thus saith our Lord God:
text.KJV: Like as they do yet this day unto my chosen, so will I do also, and recompense in their bosom. Thus saith the Lord God;
text.NRSV: Just as they have done to my elect until this day, so I will do, and will repay into their bosom. Thus says the Lord God:

text.DRB: My right hand shall not spare sinners, neither shall the sword cease upon them that shed innocent blood upon the earth.
text.KJV: My right hand shall not spare the sinners, and my sword shall not cease over them that shed innocent blood upon the earth.
text.NRSV: My right hand will not spare the sinners, and my sword will not cease from those who shed innocent blood on earth.

text.DRB: Fire came forth from his wrath, and hath devoured the foundations of the earth, and sinners as it were straw set on fire.
text.KJV: The fire is gone forth from his wrath, and hath consumed the foundations of the earth, and the sinners, like the straw that is kindled.
text.NRSV: And a fire went forth from his wrath, and consumed the foundations of the earth and the sinners, like burnt straw.

text.DRB: Woe to them that sin, and observe not my commandments, saith our Lord.
text.KJV: Woe to them that sin, and keep not my commandments! saith the Lord.
text.NRSV: Alas for those who sin and do not observe my commandments, says the Lord;]

text.DRB: I will not spare them: depart o children from the power. Defile not my sanctification:
text.KJV: I will not spare them: go your way, ye children, from the power, defile not my sanctuary.
text.NRSV: I will not spare them. Depart, you faithless children! Do not pollute my sanctuary.

text.DRB: because the Lord knoweth all that sin against him; therefore hath he delivered them into death and into slaughter.
text.KJV: For the Lord knoweth all them that sin against him, and therefore delivereth he them unto death and destruction.
text.NRSV: For God] knows all who sin against him; therefore he will hand them over to death and slaughter.

text.DRB: For now are evils come upon the world, and you shall tarry in them. For God will not deliver you, because you have sinned against him.
text.KJV: For now are the plagues come upon the whole earth and ye shall remain in them: for God shall not deliver you, because ye have sinned against him.
text.NRSV: Already calamities have come upon the whole earth, and you shall remain in them; God] will not deliver you, because you have sinned against him.

text.DRB: Behold an horrible vision, and the face of it from the east.
text.KJV: Behold an horrible vision, and the appearance thereof from the east:
text.NRSV: What a terrifying sight, appearing from the east!

text.DRB: And the nations of dragons of Arabians shall come forth in many chariots, and as a wind the number of them is carried upon the earth, so that now all do fear and tremble, that shall hear them.
text.KJV: Where the nations of the dragons of Arabia shall come out with many chariots, and the multitude of them shall be carried as the wind upon earth, that all they which hear them may fear and tremble.
text.NRSV: The nations of the dragons of Arabia shall come out with many chariots, and from the day that they set out, their hissing shall spread over the earth, so that all who hear them will fear and tremble.

text.DRB: The Carmonians mad for anger, and they shall go forth as wild boars out of the wood, and they shall come with great power, and shall stand in fight with them, and they shall waste the portion of the land of the Assyrians.
text.KJV: Also the Carmanians raging in wrath shall go forth as the wild boars of the wood, and with great power shall they come, and join battle with them, and shall waste a portion of the land of the Assyrians.
text.NRSV: Also the Carmonians, raging in wrath, shall go forth like wild boars] from the forest, and with great power they shall come and engage them in battle, and with their tusks they shall devastate a portion of the land of the Assyrians with their teeth.

text.DRB: And after these things the dragons shall prevail mindful of their nativity, & conspiring shall turn themselves in great force to pursue them.
text.KJV: And then shall the dragons have the upper hand, remembering their nature; and if they shall turn themselves, conspiring together in great power to persecute them,
text.NRSV: And then the dragons,] remembering their origin, shall become still stronger; and if they combine in great power and turn to pursue them,

text.DRB: These shall be troubled and hold their peace at their force, and shall turn their feet into flight.
text.KJV: Then these shall be troubled bled, and keep silence through their power, and shall flee.
text.NRSV: then these shall be disorganized and silenced by their power, and shall turn and flee.]

text.DRB: And from the territory of the Assyrians the besiegers shall besiege them, and shall consume one of them, and there shall be fear and trembling in their army, and contention against their kings.
text.KJV: And from the land of the Assyrians shall the enemy besiege them, and consume some of them, and in their host shall be fear and dread, and strife among their kings.
text.NRSV: And from the land of the Assyrians an enemy in ambush shall attack them and destroy one of them, and fear and trembling shall come upon their army, and indecision upon their kings.

text.DRB: Behold clouds from the east, and from the north unto the south, and their face very horrible, full of wrath and storm.
text.KJV: Behold clouds from the east and from the north unto the south, and they are very horrible to look upon, full of wrath and storm.
text.NRSV: See the clouds from the east, and from the north to the south! Their appearance is exceedingly threatening, full of wrath and storm.

text.DRB: And they shall beat one against another, & they shall beat down many stars, and their star upon the earth, and blood shall be from the sword unto the belly.
text.KJV: They shall smite one upon another, and they shall smite down a great multitude of stars upon the earth, even their own star; and blood shall be from the sword unto the belly,
text.NRSV: They shall clash against one another and shall pour out a heavy tempest on the earth, and their own tempest;] and there shall be blood from the sword as high as a horse’s belly

text.DRB: And man's dung unto the camel's litter, and there shall be much fear, and trembling upon the earth.
text.KJV: And dung of men unto the camel’s hough.
text.NRSV: and a man’s thigh and a camel’s hock.

text.DRB: And they shall shake that shall see that wrath, & tremble shall take them: and after these things there shall many showers be moved:
text.KJV: And there shall be great fearfulness and trembling upon earth: and they that see the wrath shall be afraid, and trembling shall come upon them.
text.NRSV: And there shall be fear and great trembling on the earth; those who see that wrath shall be horror-stricken, and they shall be seized with trembling.

text.DRB: from the south, and the north: and another portion from the west.
text.KJV: And then shall there come great storms from the south, and from the north, and another part from the west.
text.NRSV: After that, heavy storm-clouds shall be stirred up from the south, and from the north, and another part from the west.

text.DRB: And the winds from the east shall prevail upon it, and shall shut it up, and the clouds which he raised in wrath, and the star to make terror to the east wind, and the west shall be destroyed.
text.KJV: And strong winds shall arise from the east, and shall open it; and the cloud which he raised up in wrath, and the star stirred to cause fear toward the east and west wind, shall be destroyed.
text.NRSV: But the winds from the east shall prevail over the cloud that was] raised in wrath, and shall dispel it; and the tempest] that was to cause destruction by the east wind shall be driven violently towards the south and west.

text.DRB: And there shall be exalted great and mighty clouds full of wrath, and a star to terrify all the earth, and the inhabitants thereof, and they shall pour in upon every high and eminent place a terrible star,
text.KJV: The great and mighty clouds shall be puffed up full of wrath, and the star, that they may make all the earth afraid, and them that dwell therein; and they shall pour out over every high and eminent place an horrible star,
text.NRSV: Great and mighty clouds, full of wrath and tempest, shall rise and destroy all the earth and its inhabitants, and shall pour out upon every high and lofty place] a terrible tempest,

text.DRB: fire, and hail, & flying swords, and many waters, so that all fields also shall be filled, and all rivers with the fullness of many waters.
text.KJV: Fire, and hail, and flying swords, and many waters, that all fields may be full, and all rivers, with the abundance of great waters.
text.NRSV: fire and hail and flying swords and floods of water, so that all the fields and all the streams shall be filled with the abundance of those waters.

text.DRB: And they shall throw down cities, and walls, and mountains, and hills, and the trees of the woods, and the grass of the meadows, and their corn.
text.KJV: And they shall break down the cities and walls, mountains and hills, trees of the wood, and grass of the meadows, and their corn.
text.NRSV: They shall destroy cities and walls, mountains and hills, trees of the forests, and grass of the meadows, and their grain.

text.DRB: And they shall pass constant unto Babylon, and shall raise her.
text.KJV: And they shall go stedfastly unto Babylon, and make her afraid.
text.NRSV: They shall go on steadily to Babylon and blot it out.

text.DRB: They shall come together against her, and shall compass her, and shall pour out the star, and all wrath upon her, and the dust and smoke shall go up even into heaven, and round about shall lament her. Apoc. 18.
text.KJV: They shall come to her, and besiege her, the star and all wrath shall they pour out upon her: then shall the dust and smoke go up unto the heaven, and all they that be about her shall bewail her.
text.NRSV: They shall come to it and surround it; they shall pour out on it the tempest] and all its fury;] then the dust and smoke shall reach the sky, and all who are around it shall mourn for it.

text.DRB: And they that shall remain under her, shall serve them that terrified her.
text.KJV: And they that remain under her shall do service unto them that have put her in fear.
text.NRSV: And those who survive shall serve those who have destroyed it.

text.DRB: And thou Asia agreeing into the hope of Babylon, and the glory of her person,
text.KJV: And thou, Asia, that art partaker of the hope of Babylon, and art the glory of her person:
text.NRSV: And you, Asia, who share in the splendour of Babylon and the glory of her person—

text.DRB: woe be to thee thou wretch, because thou art like to her, and hast adorned thy daughters in fornication, to please & glory in thy lovers, which have desired always to fornicate with thee.
text.KJV: Woe be unto thee, thou wretch, because thou hast made thyself like unto her; and hast decked thy daughters in whoredom, that they might please and glory in thy lovers, which have always desired to commit whoredom with thee.
text.NRSV: woe to you, miserable wretch! For you have made yourself like her; you have decked out your daughters for prostitution to please and glory in your lovers, who have always lusted after you.

text.DRB: Thou hast imitated the odious in all her works, and in her inventions: therefore saith God:
text.KJV: Thou hast followed her that is hated in all her works and inventions: therefore saith God,
text.NRSV: You have imitated that hateful one in all her deeds and devices.] Therefore God] says,

text.DRB: I will send in evils upon thee, widowhood, poverty, and famine, and sword, and pestilence, to destroy thy houses by violation, and death, and glory of thy virtue.
text.KJV: I will send plagues upon thee; widowhood, poverty, famine, sword, and pestilence, to waste thy houses with destruction and death.
text.NRSV: I will send evils upon you: widowhood, poverty, famine, sword, and pestilence, bringing ruin to your houses, bringing destruction and death.

text.DRB: As a flower shall be withered, when the heat shall rise that is sent forth upon thee,
text.KJV: And the glory of thy Power shall be dried up as a flower, the heat shall arise that is sent over thee.
text.NRSV: And the glory of your strength shall wither like a flower when the heat shall rise that is sent upon you.

text.DRB: thou shalt be weakened as a little poor soul plagued and chastised of women, that the mighty and the lovers may not receive thee.
text.KJV: Thou shalt be weakened as a poor woman with stripes, and as one chastised with wounds, so that the mighty and lovers shall not be able to receive thee.
text.NRSV: You shall be weakened like a wretched woman who is beaten and wounded, so that you cannot receive your mighty lovers.

text.DRB: Will I be zealous against thee saith our Lord,
text.KJV: Would I with jealousy have so proceeded against thee, saith the Lord,
text.NRSV: Would I have dealt with you so violently, says the Lord,

text.DRB: unless thou hadst slain mine elect at all times, exalting the slaughter of the hands, and saying upon their death, when thou was drunken.
text.KJV: If thou hadst not always slain my chosen, exalting the stroke of thine hands, and saying over their dead, when thou wast drunken,
text.NRSV: if you had not killed my chosen people continually, exulting and clapping your hands and talking about their death when you were drunk?

text.DRB: Adorn the beauty of thy countenance.
text.KJV: Set forth the beauty of thy countenance?
text.NRSV: Beautify your face!

text.DRB: The reward of thy fornication is in thy bosom, therefore thou shalt receive recompence.
text.KJV: The reward of thy whoredom shall be in thy bosom, therefore shalt thou receive recompence.
text.NRSV: The reward of a prostitute is in your lap; therefore you shall receive your recompense.

text.DRB: And thy children shall die for famine: and thou shalt fall by the sword, and thy cities shall be destroyed, and all thine shall fall in the field by the sword.
text.KJV: Thy children shall die of hunger, and thou shalt fall through the sword: thy cities shall be broken down, and all thine shall perish with the sword in the field.
text.NRSV: Your children shall die of hunger, and you shall fall by the sword; your cities shall be wiped out, and all your people who are in the open country shall fall by the sword.

text.DRB: And they that are in the mountains shall perish with famine, and shall eat their own flesh, and drink blood, for the famine of bread and thirst of waters.
text.KJV: They that be in the mountains shall die of hunger, and eat their own flesh, and drink their own blood, for very hunger of bread, and thirst of water.
text.NRSV: Those who are in the mountains and highlands] shall perish of hunger, and they shall eat their own flesh in hunger for bread, and drink their own blood in thirst for water.

text.DRB: Unhappy by the seas shalt thou come, and again thou shalt receive evils.
text.KJV: Thou as unhappy shalt come through the sea, and receive plagues again.
text.NRSV: Unhappy above all others, you shall come and suffer fresh miseries.

text.DRB: And in the passage they shall beat against the idle city, and shall destroy some portion of thy land, and shall deface part of thy glory, again returning to Babylon overthrown.
text.KJV: And in the passage they shall rush on the idle city, and shall destroy some portion of thy land, and consume part of thy glory, and shall return to Babylon that was destroyed.
text.NRSV: As they pass by they shall crush the hateful] city, and shall destroy a part of your land and abolish a portion of your glory, when they return from devastated Babylon.

text.DRB: And being thrown down thou shalt be to them for stubble, and they shall be to thee fire:
text.KJV: And thou shalt be cast down by them as stubble, and they shall be unto thee as fire;
text.NRSV: You shall be broken down by them like stubble,] and they shall be like fire to you.

text.DRB: and devour thee, and thy cities, thy land, and thy mountains, all thy woods and fruitful trees they will burn with fire.
text.KJV: And shall consume thee, and thy cities, thy land, and thy mountains; all thy woods and thy fruitful trees shall they burn up with fire.
text.NRSV: They shall devour you and your cities, your land and your mountains; they shall burn with fire all your forests and your fruitful trees.

text.DRB: Thy children they shall lead captive, and shall have thy goods for a prey, and the glory of thy face they shall destroy.
text.KJV: Thy children shall they carry away captive, and, look, what thou hast, they shall spoil it, and mar the beauty of thy face.
text.NRSV: They shall carry your children away captive, plunder your wealth, and mar the glory of your countenance.

text.KJV: Woe be unto thee, Babylon, and Asia! woe be unto thee, Egypt and Syria!
text.NRSV: Woe to you, Babylon and Asia! Woe to you, Egypt and Syria!
text.DRB: Table OF
Gird yourselves with sackcloths and shirts of hair, and mourn for your children, and be sorry: because your destruction is at hand.
text.KJV: Gird up yourselves with cloths of sack and hair, bewail your children, and be sorry; for your destruction is at hand.
text.NRSV: Bind on sackcloth and cloth of goats’ hair,] and wail for your children, and lament for them; for your destruction is at hand.

text.DRB: The sword is sent in upon you, and who is he that can turn it away?
text.KJV: A sword is sent upon you, and who may turn it back?
text.NRSV: The sword has been sent upon you, and who is there to turn it back?

text.DRB: Fire is sent in upon you, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: A fire is sent among you, and who may quench it?
text.NRSV: A fire has been sent upon you, and who is there to quench it?

text.DRB: Evils are sent in upon you, and who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: Plagues are sent unto you, and what is he that may drive them away?
text.NRSV: Calamities have been sent upon you, and who is there to drive them away?

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the lion being hungry in the wood, or quench the fire in stubble, forthwith when it beginneth to burn?
text.KJV: May any man drive away an hungry lion in the wood? or may any one quench the fire in stubble, when it hath begun to burn?
text.NRSV: Can one drive off a hungry lion in the forest, or quench a fire in the stubble once it has started to burn?]

text.DRB: Shall any man repel the arrow shot of a strong archer?
text.KJV: May one turn again the arrow that is shot of a strong archer?
text.NRSV: Can one turn back an arrow shot by a strong archer?

text.DRB: Our strong Lord sendeth in evils, & who is he that can repel them?
text.KJV: The mighty Lord sendeth the plagues and who is he that can drive them away?
text.NRSV: The Lord God sends calamities, and who will drive them away?

text.DRB: Fire came forth from his wrath, and who is he that can quench it?
text.KJV: A fire shall go forth from his wrath, and who is he that may quench it?
text.NRSV: Fire will go forth from his wrath, and who is there to quench it?

text.DRB: He will lighten, who shall not fear, he will thunder, and who shall not be afraid?
text.KJV: He shall cast lightnings, and who shall not fear? he shall thunder, and who shall not be afraid?
text.NRSV: He will flash lightning, and who will not be afraid? He will thunder, and who will not be terrified?

text.DRB: Our Lord will threaten, and who shall not utterly be destroyed before his face?
text.KJV: The Lord shall threaten, and who shall not be utterly beaten to powder at his presence?
text.NRSV: The Lord will threaten, and who will not be utterly shattered at his presence?

text.DRB: The earth hath trembled, and the foundations thereof, the sea tosseth up waves from the depth, and the floods of it shall be destroyed, and the fishes thereof at the face of our Lord, and at the glory of his power:
text.KJV: The earth quaketh, and the foundations thereof; the sea ariseth up with waves from the deep, and the waves of it are troubled, and the fishes thereof also, before the Lord, and before the glory of his power:
text.NRSV: The earth and its foundations quake, the sea is churned up from the depths, and its waves and the fish with them shall be troubled at the presence of the Lord and the glory of his power.

text.DRB: because his right hand is strong which bendeth the bow, his arrows be sharp that are shot of him, they shall not miss, when they shall begin to be shot into the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: For strong is his right hand that bendeth the bow, his arrows that he shooteth are sharp, and shall not miss, when they begin to be shot into the ends of the world.
text.NRSV: For his right hand that bends the bow is strong, and his arrows that he shoots are sharp and when they are shot to the ends of the world will not miss once.

text.DRB: Behold evils are sent, and they shall not return till they come upon the earth.
text.KJV: Behold, the plagues are sent, and shall not return again, until they come upon the earth.
text.NRSV: Calamities are sent forth and shall not return until they come over the earth.

text.DRB: The fire is kindled and it shall not be quenched, till it consume the foundations of the earth.
text.KJV: The fire is kindled, and shall not be put out, till it consume the foundation of the earth.
text.NRSV: The fire is kindled, and shall not be put out until it consumes the foundations of the earth.

text.DRB: For as the arrow shot of a strong archer returneth not, so shall not the evils return back, that shall be sent upon the earth.
text.KJV: Like as an arrow which is shot of a mighty archer returneth not backward: even so the plagues that shall be sent upon earth shall not return again.
text.NRSV: Just as an arrow shot by a mighty archer does not return, so the calamities that are sent upon the earth shall not return.

text.DRB: Woe is me: woe is me: who shall deliver me in those days?
text.KJV: Woe is me! woe is me! who will deliver me in those days?
text.NRSV: Alas for me! Alas for me! Who will deliver me in those days?

text.DRB: The beginning of sorrows and much mourning, the beginning of famine and much destruction. The beginning of wars and the potestates shall fear, the beginning of evils and all shall tremble.
text.KJV: The beginning of sorrows and great mournings; the beginning of famine and great death; the beginning of wars, and the powers shall stand in fear; the beginning of evils! what shall I do when these evils shall come?
text.NRSV: The beginning of sorrows, when there shall be much lamentation; the beginning of famine, when many shall perish; the beginning of wars, when the powers shall be terrified; the beginning of calamities, when all shall tremble. What shall they do, when the calamities come?

text.DRB: In these what shall I do, when the evils shall come?
text.KJV: Behold, famine and plague, tribulation and anguish, are sent as scourges for amendment.
